responses.bloodline: I don't think I should have a bloodline
responses.blood_potency: Alice has not yet been a vampire for 50 years, so she would have the starting 1 dot.
responses.gaming_style_note: I wouldn't feel confident in being anything major, but I think causing chaos is always fun, and I enjoy helping people achieve their goals.
responses.support_note: I have never played this game before, or done any LARPing, so I will be very ignorant, sorry! I am reading the rule books, but I would appreciate assistance/guidance when I am accidentally using the rules wrong, breaking LARPing etiquette, or floundering.
responses.covenant_factions: The circle of the crone is where Alice is leaning, although I don't know if she should be in it yet or not
responses.court_motivation: Knowledge and a foothold. She wants to know what is going on and if it can be used to get more information. Also her sire disappeared and she thinks it might be because her sire found out some info they weren't supposed to or potentially her sire was just insane.
responses.ambitions_sydney: Permanent home, she is actually terrified of planes and ships
responses.why_sydney: Was embraced here
responses.why_covenant: Sire was part of the covenant before sire went missing. The sire talked a lot about how they Embraced Alice so she could help them uncover "the truth", although that might have been just talk
responses.covenant_goals: secrets, and to discover what happened to their sire and/or if their sire was delusional
responses.clan_goals: Hoping for uniting clan-mates, but suspicious of them
responses.aspired_position: Primogen would be the closest
responses.view_traditions: She thinks they are sensible, but doesn't know too much about the finer details or history of them
responses.view_elysium: She honours it for now, but if she sees others break it and get away with it, that might change her thoughts
responses.view_mortals: She is still quite close to mortals, and is very uncomfortable around ghouls because of the slavery parallels.
responses.embrace_story: She was at a new year's eve party, talking about Y2K and whether or not the world was about to end to someone she had been chatting to on a BBS forum. The person was asking her all kinds of questions about her beliefs and thoughts on the world ending etc. Then they asked her if she wanted to have a chance to discover more about the truth of the world and Alice said yes, although she was expecting conspiracy stuff, not vampires.
responses.sire_story: Missing. They were someone who Alice met online talking about various conspiracy theories. They were/are obsessed with finding out some greater truth, and they needed someone who had greater aptitude with technology to deep dive into things. They went missing shortly after Alice was Embraced, but before they disappeared they spent a lot of time sounding very paranoid, and that they were close to a "big truth". Alice could never discover if this was true, and they were disappeared because they knew too much, or if they were suffering from delusions and simply wandered off somewhere or got themselves killed.
responses.early_nights: Sydney during New Years Eve about to hit 2000
responses.last_city_politics: Not applicable, I don't think.
responses.touchstones: Currently her family is her main one. Her parents, and her sister. She also has a connection to her local library, and to the online forums she still frequents.
responses.hunting_style_note: She spent a lot of time trying to figure out how to do the pop culture blood bank route before panicking and going to king's cross and eating any men she saw trying to have sex with women too drunk to say yes. Nowadays she more hunts down people who post awful things online and eats them.
responses.first_kill: She was hunting in Kings Cross and frenzied. She then panicked and hid the corpse on a rooftop.
responses.common_indulgences: Books, and knowledge. Also, arguing on the internet.
responses.allies: I don't know anyone else
responses.coterie: I don't know anyone else
responses.enemies: Not to my knowledge
responses.opposed_covenant: She doesn't yet know much about them
responses.intolerable_behaviours: She is against slavery, including ghouls, anything involving torture or sexual harm is right out.

mortal_family.description: Relatives are still alive, they don't know she's dead, she emails them a lot in the middle of the night

responses.apparent_age: Late teens to very early twenties
responses.gaming_style_note: Some mysticism and stuff like that? Anichka is not designed for PVP, and will probably be a tool, hindrance, or confidante to some.
responses.support_note: Some prophecies actually being useful and coming to pass- gives the character legitimacy, and keeps her fun
responses.court_motivation: She has visions, and she is driven to seek out the players in them. She wants to know about this strange place, and when she sees all the people that are here, she will need to work out what they're all doing here together
responses.ambitions_sydney: At the moment, Sydney is a safe place for her family. She hopes to eventually return to Ukraine with her family, but knows that may not be possible.
responses.why_sydney: Family needed an out, and possibly vision/instruction from older Crone (at ST discretion)
responses.why_covenant: Because when her mother was dying, she called out to God for help and no one answered, When she herself was dying, she called out to anyone listening, and the Mother sent her Sire and her brood brother to save her.
responses.covenant_goals: To assist, to mentor, to spread the visions, to look for seers to come and guide them.
responses.clan_goals: She's not got a lot to say about her Clan. She's vaguely proud of the shadows in her blood and the secrets that whisper, but it doesn't really occur to her night to nnight. Maybe that will change.
responses.aspired_position: I guess this one is a Socialite, as a seer/oracle, potentially a sacred judge?
responses.view_traditions: In spite of her preference for structure and stability, she's surprisingly loose on all three of the Traditions. Not that she'd let anyone know all that >.>
responses.view_elysium: Elysium is probably the 'tradition' she cares for the most. A key point of sanctuary
responses.view_mortals: Tools, petitioners, potential points of reference. Everything and everyone can teach her something. She's currently on animal blood so she can get her haven set up safely, but gosh it would be nice to go back there >.>
responses.embrace_story: She was half insane and dead when she was Embraced. She was liberated from an emptying worker's camp in WW2,
responses.sire_story: Anichka just refers to him as 'my sire'. She sees him like a wind spirit or dryad- a lauging, free spirited thing, who slips between shadows, steals, learns secrets, and does the will of the Goddess.
responses.early_nights: Anichka was left staked for about a decade after her Embrace, due to not being able to process the extreme conditions around her in the work camp. The stake actually grew into a tree around her, and that's part of how she regained her chill. A lot of her first nights awake were spent traveling, walking-talking education with her brood brother and sire, active demonstrations of powers and skills, and little contact with courts.
responses.last_city_politics: Anichka was part of a couple of Soviet-bloc courts in the sixties and seventies, favouring Crone-heavy cities, and stifling under high secular praxes. Mostly she wandered, though- again, favouring Crone and Carthian cities, tolerating Invictus ones, and trying to avoid Sanctified ones- all dictated by her intuition and visions. Her view of court structures is very cosmopolitan.
responses.touchstones: One of her Touchstone is the current youngest among her family, named after her mother, Kateryna
responses.hunting_style_note: She mostly eats animals. Sometimes she'll jump and scrag someone if she's in desparate neeed, but she prefers to cultivate her territory and ensure she can eat at home to the north.
responses.common_indulgences: She likes to wander, cloud chase, or just lay still in the dirt and leaf litter. Crawl among the roots of trees. Be still. She's trying to remember what her father taught her about carving from deadfall she finds. It's not going as well as she'd like.
responses.allies: Anichka has given prophecy to The keeper, Rene Meyer, Ivana and (Kurtis's PC), and currently negotiating with others. She will be glad to see them, and they will probably cautiously optimistic to see her.
responses.coterie: Not yet, but we've started talking about it, and will form one in play!
responses.enemies: Currently negotiating an almost one sided antipathy towards Cyrus, over him delivering news about her brood brother.
responses.opposed_covenant: She really quite dislikes the LS, and rolls her eyes at the Invictus, mostly due to armed conflict between them and some European crone in the mid seventies, as well as, you know, just clashing on a social level.
responses.intolerable_behaviours: Excessive oppression of religious expression, especially towards her own Covenant. People who betray the Crone. Very quick temper over unfounded statements that 'the Crone isn't like that'.

mortal_family.description: Anichka has cultivated her mortal family into a sort of support nework-cum-cult. Late teens and adults have vague awarenesses that something is Not Right about Auntie Anichka, but the children are left out of that business.

boons_debts.description: Don't have those, but maybe some of the people she's given prophecy thnk she owes them an explanation or more information

responses.gaming_style_note: Happy to be a part of the political game both high and low. Haven't LARPed before; but am interested in learning and having the opportunity to engage.
responses.support_note: I tend to get overwhelmed; I need assistance with combat and political mechanics.
responses.bloodline_rationale: Brandy was Embraced as an act of calculated cruelty and political revenge by an Invictus Gangrel of a Daimonion lineage that embodies corruption hidden beneath perfect civility. Expected to break, she instead survived and was taken in by the Circle of the Crone, who saw her suffering and forced transformation as sacred. The bloodline enhances her story by defining her central struggle ? resisting the controlled monstrosity her sire represents and proving that imposed corruption does not have to define who she becomes.
responses.court_motivation:
1. To increase the political power of the Crone
2. To accrue accolades and status
3. To thwart the efforts of the Invictus
responses.ambitions_sydney:
By achieving my ambitions I hope to:
1. Infuriate my Invictus Sire
2. Make Mother proud
responses.why_sydney: The opportunity was provided by my Hierophant to prove myself; and to move me out of harms way from my cruel sire
responses.why_covenant: Brandy did not choose the Circle of the Crone at first ? she was found by them after surviving an Embrace meant to destroy her. They were the first to interpret her suffering as transformation rather than failure, and the first to offer meaning instead of control. What began as refuge became genuine belief; their teachings gave her a way to reclaim her unlife from the cruelty that created it. Where once she was made to suffer for someone else?s revenge and power, she now endures hardship by her own will, as part of her own growth and transformation. She remains because they see her survival as sacred ? and because they taught her that what she became does not have to belong to her sire.
responses.covenant_goals:
Brandi seeks understanding, mastery, and spiritual strength within the Circle of the Crone. She wants to deepen her connection to the Dark Mother and learn the rites and mysteries that help her control the Beast without denying it. The Circle gave meaning to her suffering, and she intends to earn her place through devotion, endurance, and transformation.

She is not driven by status or political power, but by personal sovereignty ? the ability to define herself rather than be defined by her sire or her creation. In time, she hopes to become a trusted ritualist or spiritual vessel, someone who guides others through suffering into transformation, just as she was guided.

For now, she sees herself as a devoted initiate still learning ? but one day, she intends to stand as living proof that survival can become sacred power.
responses.clan_goals: Irrelevant to Australia, but I would like to see the utter obliteration of one specific Gangrel Lineage. Mine.
responses.aspired_position: Socialite
responses.view_traditions:
Brandy sees most traditions and laws as necessary structures, not sacred truths. Having been created as a tool of someone else?s power, she is wary of anything presented as absolute or unquestionable. She respects rules that preserve stability, protect the vulnerable, and prevent senseless cruelty ? especially those that keep predators from turning on their own kind without consequence.

However, she does not view hierarchy itself as holy. She has seen how propriety and ?civilized order? can mask calculated brutality. Laws that exist only to maintain dominance or protect the powerful earn her compliance, but not her reverence.

She respects the Masquerade and the need for discipline; she fears the unchecked Beast ? both her own and others?. What she truly distrusts are systems that demand obedience without meaning. Sacred law, to her, must serve transformation and balance ? not vanity or revenge.
responses.view_elysium: Brandi respects the sanctity of Elysium as a necessary boundary, not a sacred one. She honours its rules genuinely because she understands the danger of predators without restraint ? including herself. However, she also recognises that Elysium?s civility is a performance, where power and intimidation still move beneath polite silence. She does not see it as cowardice, but as containment ? a fragile peace that must be maintained, even if it is never entirely honest.
responses.view_mortals:
Brandy views mortals as necessary and fragile ? not equals, but not meaningless. They are sustenance, yes, but also reminders of who she was before her unlife was used against her. She feeds with restraint when she can, and avoids cruelty for its own sake.

She is deeply uncomfortable with the idea of ownership. Having been created as a weapon of revenge, she refuses to treat ghouls as property. If she were to keep one, it would be by choice and consent, not domination ? and she would see them as an ally under protection rather than a servant under control.
responses.embrace_story:
Brandy?s Embrace was cold, deliberate, and controlled ? cruelty performed with perfect manners. She did not know it was coming; she was taken, drained slowly, and forced to experience every moment of fear and helplessness while her sire remained composed and precise. There was no frenzy, no passion ? only calculated intent.

She died terrified and confused, and rose in shock, hunger, and animal panic. Her first instinct was to fight, flee, and survive ? not to understand. Even now, she remembers the calmness of her sire more vividly than the pain, and that controlled cruelty haunts her more than the death itself.
responses.sire_story:
Sire: Colton Beauregard Price ? active.

An Invictus Gangrel of a Daimonion lineage, Colton Embraced Brandi as an act of calculated revenge and political cruelty, intending her transformation to be a living punishment inflicted on another. He values absolute control, immaculate propriety, and domination concealed beneath civility.

Their relationship now is one of rejection and quiet tension. He considers her a failed instrument that survived beyond its purpose; she refuses his claim over her existence. They do not interact openly, but his shadow still shapes her unlife ? and she fully expects that one night, he will come to reclaim what he believes is his.
responses.early_nights:
Brandy?s Embrace was cold, deliberate, and controlled ? cruelty performed with perfect manners. She did not know it was coming. She was taken to her sire?s Texas torture ranch, drained slowly, and forced to endure every moment of fear and helplessness while he remained composed and precise. There was no frenzy, no passion ? only calculated intent.

She died terrified and confused, and rose in shock, hunger, and animal panic. Her first nights were spent in that place of control and suffering, but her true turning came later in the Louisiana bayou, where the Circle of the Crone found her. There, in the wet earth and living dark, her unlife was no longer punishment but transformation. Even now, she remembers the calm cruelty of her sire ? but it is the bayou that taught her how to survive what he made her into.
responses.last_city_politics:
From the bayou, Brandy knew a domain shaped less by formal rule and more by ancient presence and quiet spiritual authority. The Circle of the Crone did not dominate politically in the traditional sense, but the land itself ? and those who spoke for it ? commanded deep respect. Power was diffuse, rooted in territory, ritual, and survival rather than titles or courts.

Kindred who remained long learned to move carefully, honour old boundaries, and listen when the Crone?s devotees spoke. Disputes were settled through negotiation, omen, or endurance rather than decree. It was not orderly, but it was balanced ? a tense harmony between predators who understood that the swamp remembers everything.
responses.touchstones:
1. Brandy keeps a small alligator in a reinforced tank filled with mud and dark water from the Louisiana bayou where she was spiritually reborn. She cares for it meticulously. The alligator represents patience, stillness, and controlled power ? the version of predation she chooses rather than the kind her sire embodied. It waits. It does not posture. It does not pretend civility. It simply is what it is.

2. When Brandy came to Australia, she did not leave her past behind ? she recreated it. On the outskirts of the city, tucked into an industrial fringe where no one asks many questions, she keeps a small, weathered trailer she purchased and modified to resemble the one she grew up in. It is the only place she allows herself to exist without performance. She visits it when the weight of politics, ritual, and restraint becomes too much. She sits on the worn steps. Listens to the quiet. Remembers being human, being poor, being powerless.
responses.hunting_style_note:
Brandy feeds primarily from a small devotional cult that gathers around her as a spiritual vessel of transformation. They come willingly, viewing the sharing of blood as sacred participation rather than exploitation. Feeding is ritualised, controlled, and never taken beyond what she believes they can safely give. She does not dominate them through fear ? she maintains strict boundaries and responsibility for their well-being. 

Outside the cult, she hunts rarely, and only when ritual feeding cannot sustain her. When she does, she follows her Gangrel instincts ? patient, silent, and deliberate ? seeking isolated places where predator and prey can meet without spectacle or humiliation. She does not charm or toy with her quarry. She stalks, chooses carefully, and ends the hunt quickly and cleanly. To Brandy, this is not cruelty but sacred necessity. The Circle taught her that predation is part of the natural order ? a cycle of life, death, and transformation. In those moments, she does not pretend to be civilised, nor does she revel in violence. She honours the truth of what she is: a predator taking what is needed to endure.
responses.first_kill:
Her first kill was not an accident ? it was a ritual hunt.

Under a blood moon, Brandy ran with a Circle pack in a sacred frenzy, the air thick with omen and hunger. The hunt was deliberate, communal, and sanctioned ? a rite marking her passage into the covenant. In that state, instinct and ritual became one, and the prey did not survive.
responses.common_indulgences: Motivational Speaking, Occult Worshipping, Fanatic Orgies, Whiskey Tastings, Opera, and Demo Derbies.
responses.allies: In discusssion
responses.coterie: In discussion
responses.enemies: In discussion
responses.opposed_covenant:
Brandy does not actively campaign against any covenant, but she is deeply distrustful of the Invictus. Their obsession with hierarchy, ownership, and perfected civility mirrors the philosophy of her sire ? power maintained through control, appearances, and calculated domination. She has seen firsthand how refinement can be used to conceal cruelty, and she rejects any system that treats people as instruments to be arranged, traded, or broken.

She does not seek open conflict with them, but she quietly resists their influence wherever it reinforces ownership over autonomy. More than anything, she wants their belief ? that power justifies control ? to fail.
responses.intolerable_behaviours:
Brandy does not tolerate cruelty disguised as refinement ? calculated harm performed calmly, politely, or for demonstration of power. She has no patience for Kindred who treat suffering as entertainment, status, or proof of superiority.

She is especially angered by:
- treating mortals or ghouls as disposable property
- domination for humiliation rather than necessity
- feeding used as punishment, spectacle, or control
- manipulation hidden behind ?civilised? manners
- violation of sacred spaces, rites, or offered trust
- predation without restraint or responsibility

She can accept that vampires must hunt. She cannot accept predators who refuse accountability for what they are.

mortal_family.description:
Some of Brandy?s mortal family are still alive in the South. Officially, she is dead ? a quiet disappearance that no one with power ever bothered to investigate too closely. They believe she ran off, or met a bad end. No body was ever found.

She does not contact them. She does not watch them closely either ? at least not often. The temptation is there, but she keeps her distance out of protection rather than indifference. Her sire taught her what it means to turn a person into leverage, and she refuses to let her family become that.

They remain a painful reminder of who she was and what was taken, but also of what she fights to preserve within herself.

boons_debts.description: In discussion.

responses.gaming_style_note: Political and Social intrigue is what I?m gonna focus on. Y?all know I?ve played this game a LONG time. I wanna play a character that puts others in the best position to thrive. So I?m gonna play a ?power behind the throne? type.
responses.support_note: I think I?m ok on this one guys, but if you think the Lance could use some herding, I can seed that.
responses.bloodline_rationale: Lasombra have always been my favourite Bloodline, it?s been something I?ve wanted to play in a Court setting, which I never got the chance to do in BTS. This gives me that chance.
responses.court_motivation: Carver attends Court to keep his finger on the pulse of the Lance and their political and social machinations, so as to make sure they ren?t going to do anything that brings the entire covenant into a shitstorm they can?t handle. He also knows lots of shit, and will be a bit of a "wizened" type for them to bounce ideas off of.
responses.ambitions_sydney: Sydney is fresh territory. Being a Lasombra, he sees it as a place he can put down roots and try to reclaim a bunch of lost Lore, beit LS or otherwise.
responses.why_sydney: There are surely a lot of relics and artefacts that were left behind by the previous Damned occupation. Carver is going to go looting.
responses.why_covenant: He was given to the Lance by his Invictus sire, as is tradition among his Lineage He just decided to stay.
responses.covenant_goals: To ensure their survival, whatever that takes. If LS are doing stupid shit... well... time to show them the Find Out side of FAFO.
responses.clan_goals: To demonstrate that true Power comes in many forms. God is the Ultimate, but we are His instruments on Earth, and we should make sure folks remember why they are here.
responses.aspired_position: Primogen
responses.view_traditions: They are the fundamentals that underpin Kindred and Damned society.
responses.view_elysium: Yes, he does. Though in some cases, exceptions need be made. To protect Elysium or enforce it.
responses.view_mortals: They are all of those things. Tools to be used, succour provided by God and a reminder that our divine purpose is to keep the Darkness Dark and guide them towards the Light.
responses.embrace_story: Carver discovered a mortal colleague embezzling funds from their mortal business venture and confronted them but was mortally wounded by a gunshot to the chest. His sire embraced him to save his life.
responses.sire_story: Violet West - Active in the Court of London. They embraced Carver and then, in the tradition of their Lineage, sent the Childer to the Church for education. Carver decided to stay, rather than return to the Invictus. Their relationship is amicable.
responses.early_nights: London, UK, being a staunch traditional Court, taught Carver the fundamentals of Kindred society. Traditions matter. Without them we are rabid Beasts. Civilisation among Damned is paramount.
responses.last_city_politics: Thats really up to you guys, I would think, given I imagine quite a few characters might choose London as their point of Origin.
responses.touchstones: Descendants of the Crowley family have moved to Sydney well in advance of Carver being able to move himself. He will be keeping a regular eye on them. One of them is an aspiring Solicitor, and Carver takes time out to tutor them as part of a "Scholarship" provided by the Church.
responses.hunting_style_note: Carver hunts those who prey on the Faithful. Be those garden variety criminals or bad apples among the Flock. He uses his powers so subdue his victims, then feeds.
responses.first_kill: Carver?s first kill was a Criminal his sire set him upon. He lost himself to the Beast, and enjoyed the power so let it happen. His sire let it happen too, but made him follow the consequences of doing so, so that he could learn what a lack of restraint causes. Ever since, he has been methodical in his feeding.
responses.common_indulgences: Carver?s indulgence is that he is a thief. He enjoys the thrill of it. He keeps a collection of random items he has acquired over the years. Some secret, others not so much.
responses.allies: He will have connections with extant Lance and Ventrue PCs, those are constantly evolving. At the very least he has ties with those PCs talking about London, and Bill's PC (sort of).
responses.coterie: Yes. Marni and Tiana's PCs.
responses.enemies: Not yet, but i'm looking for one. I'll feel one out and approach a player after a few games to establish one.
responses.opposed_covenant: The Carthians represent societal change and chaos, so Carver views them with disdain. He accepts the Crone have faith, which puts them above the Carthians, but only just.
responses.intolerable_behaviours: Impulsive killing, revelry in the Beast, a lack of respect for the Traditions.

mortal_family.description: Carver had no Children, however he did watch his mortal family while still in London at the behest of his Sire. He was middle aged when he was embraced, so kept in touch for a little while before staging his death in the late 60s. After that it became an academic exercise to identify Scions for the embrace as and when the Lineage needed them.

boons_debts.description: I havent nailed this down yet, but there will be favours owed to and from Carver.

responses.high_concept: Mad Dog - once they find interest in something, they won't let go
responses.gaming_style_note: I would like to play this character as more a wildcard I think, but one thats consistent. Like once others get to know my character, they know what their deal is as a basis, but may not exactly want to deal with it - or perhaps direct my character at enemies if the situations suits (I the player don't think this character would mind playing a pawn sometimes, if they get something out of it!). Not sure about competitive play, but my character's drive is knowledge - if they learn something new, and, if possible, of signifigance, then that is a night well spent.
responses.support_note: Def may need help with character connections and social dynamics of court, at least at the start. My LARP experience is usually with more passive characters, watching more than taking action. But I'd like to try to be more forward with anything my character experiences.
responses.covenant_factions: No faction just yet from mmory
responses.bloodline_rationale: The concept of my character includes the 'lunacy' of a Malkovian - a dogged vampire who in life, likely wasn't so outwardly deranged - but the Embrace has released them of such shackles, and now, they have the freedom to find out exactly how things work, in whatever ways needed. Mortality not required.
responses.court_motivation: Pure curiousity. When will they ever get the chance to see Kindred Society be build from the ground up again like this? And then have the opportunity to potentially influence a few things, sew a few seeds if possible and see what happens.
responses.ambitions_sydney: To establish new territory in a brand new place, a safe place for themselves - and then maybe see where things go. They don't have any initial drive to climb any political ladders, but they understand that may be a neccessary evil.
responses.why_sydney: As with Court, pure curiousity. A new territory, away from the powers that already have such machinations that it is an absolute mountain to discover, let alone analyse, take apart and put back together again. So many new things to discover and understand the workings of.
responses.why_covenant: Every other Covenant seemed so... in their own way. Individuality is lost in such groups, how can one even think for themselves when the very basis of their ideals are set in unmoving stone? It is a curiousity. Vampires also tend to really like to war with each other. At the very least, Carthians accept each other's existance as a basis, and listen to each individual. Within reason.
responses.covenant_goals: Secrets to guard, if they are known. Secrets to share, if the information given in kind is worthy of it. Cazz sees themselves as a bit of an outlier, but, they will be loyal, as needed.
responses.clan_goals: Certainly do not like the social climbing that most Ventrue seem to excel at, and be known for. Perhaps it comes with their Bloodline, or perhaps comes from their mortal days as a reseacher with little social life. But, to be social is to manipulate and twist things ever so slightly to ones favour, and so, knowing other Ventrue is still something that is needed.
responses.aspired_position: No real drive to pull for formal authority - but getting close to some to find out how things work, maybe.
responses.view_traditions: These laws do exist for a reason - and these reasons ave been tested time and time again. Cazz does respect rules that are in place for a reason. But, was not their Bloodline created from an 'unsanctioned' Embrace? Was that how the story goes? In general, breaking these rules are not something Cazz would think of as a first response. Or a second, or a third, or fourth. But, they are not off the table. And, if any are on the table, such actions would not be made unless there was a very, VERY good reason.
responses.view_elysium: Absolutely follow the rules, mostly genuinely - but if someone else is poked and prodded enough to break them, why, would it not be fun to find that threshold? Kindred do like to hold onto the idea of civility, so, one can play along, when needed.
responses.view_mortals: Fascinating little things they are, and oh so very loyal, when one makes sure they know only what they need to know. Cazz mostly sees them as tools and things to use where needed. But, as with any tool, they do need to be looked after to remain usable, and in good working order.
responses.embrace_story: They did not know it was coming - one late night in France when research ran late into the night. Cazz knew of their Sire - but they were not friends, colleagues, at best.
responses.sire_story: Elliot Beaux - missing. He thought he could 'have' someone to share their nights with, if not in work, then now, in Blood. But, the Embrace changed Cazz, and brought out their now shared Derangement. This mirror into himself had Elliot abandon Cazz some years later. Cazz never finished their University studies due to this, although continued to work where possible until their distracted research and lack of aging got to the edge of breaking the Masquerade. Cazz used to keep an ear out for their Sire regularly. But, as the world kept turning and other things took their attention, they actively looked for him less and less. But, they still keep an ear out. Just in case.
responses.early_nights: France, Paris - it was where they were studying and working at the time, and where they first came into contact with the Carthian Movement.
responses.touchstones:
Their main Touchstone is the University of Paris (specifically the section that in Modern Day is the Paris-East Crteil University). A reminder of what they were once striving for in life and learning. They travel often enough, but, always ended up back in Paris for a while, to the University.

They have had some further touchstones in the form of students there over the years, and have followed a few from afar - seeing when they achieve, when they fail, where they go in their short, mortal lives. But it doesn't last - sometimes, Cazz doesn't even know their names - as there is always a new student with a new thirst for learning. It is pretty much the only thing in their unlife that is a constant.
responses.allies: (I believe this is player name) Brendan. A Mekhet, we have a mutually beneficial relationship. Cazz acts as the jingly keys to distract others when Brendan wants to find things out. Cazz allows this as they also usually get information from the situation, and from the other vampire.
responses.coterie: No coterie as of yet.

responses.blood_potency: I am happy with blood potency 1
responses.gaming_style_note: In the previous chronicle, I played an extremely antisocial and investigative character (though I never seemed to successfully investigate anything). This time around, I would like to be involved in the social manoeuvring and scheming. I enjoy most genres of horror as both a victim and a cause, and in creating characters I like adding the flair of functionally useless flavour-text. Personal drama which doesn't always end in betrayal would be interesting and a different perspective this time around.
responses.support_note:
I would like to be able to attend a game session, enjoy the event, buzz about it a little with friends afterwards and then go home with readiness to enjoy the next session. The previous chronicle required a constant "in" via your covenant and to investigate plot beats in specific ways, or they would be locked out of major plot. I struggled to make my character feel useful in the group while also feeling unable to pursue "my own fun" because of the dead ends my character would meet during Downtime activities. 

If it is possible to casually enjoy the game, I would like to do so. As I would like to try playing a high-charisma character, I would appreciate some guidance into the social ken and systems of the game so I can accurately use them.
responses.bloodline_rationale: I would like to play a high-social character that is part of the Circle of the Crone. The Gorgons, as a Ventue bloodline with lore of being power-hungry and slightly delusional of their own grandeur seemed like the perfect choice. Additionally, I like the snake aesthetics/themes of the Gorgon bloodline and find the aesthetic and motifs of a character one of my main fascinations in making + designing them.
responses.court_motivation: Charles is motivated by the typical hunger for power and influence that characterises his bloodline. He is attending court to make a name for himself, seeking allies, connections and influence.
responses.ambitions_sydney: Charles hopes to claim territory and prestige in Sydney. In America, he was an underling of his sire and had been plotting their demise & his hostile takeover of the Cult. Venturing to Sydney is an opportunity to get out from under her thumb and carve his own legacy. Charles moved to Australia with his wife and son, who is his major touchstone with humanity.
responses.why_sydney: Charles was recruited into an American pentacostal cult as a human and eventually Embraced into the "true" faith an of it by the vampiric sire who led the cult. Sydney presents an opportunity to make a name for himself and claim independence from his sire by centring a new cult around him.
responses.why_covenant: Charles was embraced into the Circle of the Crone and is more than happy to use its magics and influence to further his own base of power. He genuinely believes in the mythology & faith of the cult he was a part of as a result of conditioning and indoctrination tactics he was subjected to in America. Since he was embraced, Charles has realised how these mythologies empower select groups and results in continuing the cycle of abuse. .
responses.covenant_goals: Charles would like to be in charge. He would be pursuing authority and control.
responses.clan_goals: He wants respect as a clan figure. Charles was probably badly emasculated by his sire and harbours a deep resentment & hidden insecurity about it.
responses.aspired_position: Primogen, and the role of a high socialite.
responses.view_traditions: The traditions are important and need to be upheld for the practical function. But.... there should be a few new ones every now-and-then.
responses.view_elysium: Charles is not a violent person and appreciates the dedicated safe-zones. He views anyone that resorts to violence as unintelligent and they should be barred from civilised/complex discussions be default.
responses.view_mortals: There are more mortals then there are kindred in this city; we need them for a variety of things including food, shelter and maintaining our sanity. They are tools and pets. They can be beloved tools and pets, but ultimately they are less than vampires who are simply destined for greatness. He is also a hypocrite.
responses.embrace_story: Charles was embraced during a cultish ceremony of "Embrace" into the "true" faith of a Cult he has joined when he was human. It was a semi-private ceremony of initiation observed by other blood-siblings or ghouls that the Sire had brought into the "True" faith (satanism with a bent of the mother and other crone lore). Charles has since been doing his sire's bidding and helping tend to the flock while adored by his wife who was made a ghoul shortly after their son was born.
responses.sire_story:
Grace Gardener. Active. Known as "the Gardener" 
Grace is the leader of a satanic cult masquerading as a pentacostal commune somewhere in the Bible Belt of American states (either Texas or Alabama). She promotes self-actualisation, mastery of nature, dedication of the body and instinctive action. Humans in this cult are encouraged to leave behind their previous lives and to give their hearts, minds and bodies to their deity. She performs frequent snake-handling sermons for her flock and entrusts a selection of "Embraced" to care for and utilise the skills of the Faithful for the security of the group. 

Charles was initiated into the "Embraced" in the 2010s when he was brought into the inner circle of his Sire's cult. He was initiated into the inner mysteries of the kindred and true faith  after serving time as a loyal human to her cult. Grace controlled her progeny and intentionally kept them weaker than her and Charles played at whatever she wanted to move upwards in her favour before he slithered his way into being sent to Sydney to claim new territory. He hates her, and has never quite forgiven the deception and manipulation of his faith despite now using it as his main tactic to feed. In retaliation, Charles aims to better and secure whatever territory he holds so that such manipulation is unnecessary
responses.early_nights: Somewhere in the American south. A cult/commune outside of a major city in Texas or Alabama - somewhere in the Bible Belt of the US.
responses.last_city_politics: A lance & invictus stronghold with conservative politics and an authoritarian bent/iron grip on mortal systems in the city. The Crone, as a covenant, had to be polite and meek to avoid being crushed by the Lance.
responses.touchstones:
Charles' major touchstone is his family. He met his wife as a young man in the Cult they were both part of, where they quickly married and had a child. Soon after their son was born, Charles was Embraced into the true faith/vampiric lifestyle and his wife was honoured/delusional enough to support the change/apparent promotion of her husband. Moving to Sydney is a challenge for the family, but brings great opportunity as well! 

His son Ezekiel is his major connection to humanity. When his son was around 12, Charles attempted to turn him into a ghoul but couldn?t do it. Since then, his son never quite forgave his father for brainwashing his mother and for being a literal monster despite how he ?loves? his family. To avoid jeopardising the masquerade, Zeke has been conditioned via dominate not to speak about his vampiric father, but otherwise retains free, human will. This has led to a tumultuous relationship made worse by the family?s move across hemispheres. 

Now 16, Zeke has been sent to an expensive boarding school in Sydney while Charles and his wife get their new commune/parish set up. He resents his father for his monstrous nature, but benefits from the wealth and insulation of the cult while enjoying the glimpses of humanity and love that peek through - almost enough to feel like a normal family.
responses.hunting_style_note: Cultivation is the major hunting techniques Charles uses. He primarily feeds on the faithful of his cult with select other outings and and visits where a little bit of charm and Dominate come in handy.
responses.first_kill:
Early after his embrace. His sire would have taken her posse to the local Court to introduce them in accordance with the local laws/permissions. During these nights, he and another Gorgon embraced at a similar time would have been out hunting and together they would have killed someone. 
Both were punished by his sire Grace and required to publicly apologise/kneel and ask forgiveness from the major figures of Court. While the official period of shame/exclusion only lasted for a year or so, Charles had not returned to that Court since. Sydney is a fresh start and opportunity to grow his influence and to never be publicly shamed ever again.
responses.common_indulgences:
As is habitual to the Gorgons, Charles likes working with clay and stone. He participates in artisanal markets which are exclusively at night. Part of his rural estate includes a workshop, kiln and deposits of wild clay. Charles uses these skills and facilities to make ritual items, talismans, artwork for auction, etc. I've reflected this with a Crafts 3 and specialty in sculpting on his character sheet. 

He hates gardening and categorically refuses to cultivate plants. The grounds and grasses of his estate are unkept; full of weeds and wildflowers. 

They are also absolutely infested by snakes.
responses.allies: I appear to have allies within the Crone, but nothing has been solidified with other crone PCs
responses.coterie: Not yet? I'm hoping to form one with Bill and another Crone player (running a "Double D's" cult of powerful women?) whose name I don't remember.
responses.enemies: None that I know of! But I suspect the lance will despise Charles within 0.5 seconds of knowing him. Charles' power base is based on
responses.opposed_covenant: Not sure yet, probably the Lance.
responses.intolerable_behaviours:
Charles despises public shaming and physical harm. Methods of flogging or other kinds of flagellation are dogmatic and tired, and should be relegated to repressed monks of the 10th century where they belong. 

He also finds public executions distasteful, as well as any performance of self-harm to indicate penance.

mortal_family.description: Yes! Charles brought his ghouled wife and teenage son with him to Sydney. His wife tend to his cult during the day while his son Ezekiel has been sent to an expensive boarding school to keep his distant from the fallout of his vampiric father?s political schemes.

boons_debts.description: Working on it! I would love some suggestions or help once Charles actually has skills that could be useful for others to know.

responses.apparent_age: Indeterminate. So inhuman it hardly matters. Embraced in his forties.
responses.gaming_style_note: The players having a banging time. Also dancing.
responses.support_note: Good lumbar support?
responses.bloodline_rationale: I just like to be a grotesque. Okay, there's more, but I'll do that later.
responses.court_motivation: The First Estate is and must remain above reproach. The only way that will persist is if we watch ourselves even more carefully than we watch the Carthian Movement. I attend Court to keep watch over my covenant mates and ensure that those who break with their vows are deadly with accordingly.
responses.ambitions_sydney: Become once again an integral part of the murkier parts of the Sydney underworld. Rebuild standing amongst the various criminal-adjacent organisations. Find the centre of the web.
responses.why_sydney: Following order - somebody has to keep the First Estate pure in these badlands, and it's not going to be any of those fancy lords.
responses.why_covenant: I was embraced into the Order of Sir Martin, then inducted into the Invictus within only weeks of my embrace. It is all I have ever known. I am bound by unbreakable oath to the Order, and therefore to my duty to the first estate.
responses.covenant_goals: He exists a little lateral of the ordinary covenant hierarchy. His goals revolve around cultivating fear and informants, and ensuring he is able to do what is necessary.
responses.clan_goals: Almost entirely indifferent to clan. Any ties of blood are useful inasmuch as they may further facilitate his work, but that is all. To that end, he will seek to forge alliances with any of his clan who seem open to the idea. Given his bloodline, a leadership position seems almost entirely unattainable, but if the opportunity presents then that would be a useful tool.
responses.aspired_position: Within the covenant, Enforcer. In the City proper, to be beneath notice.
responses.view_traditions: The traditions are vital. Anyone caught breaching them must be dealt with swiftly and severely. Of course, he may have to ignore the traditions himself, but that is central - I sin so that others do not have to.
responses.view_elysium: Alrways the letter, never the spirit
responses.view_mortals: No ghouls. That's just rude. Anything and everything outside the First Estate that is not a tool or an obstacle to his purpose can and will be ignored.
responses.touchstones: Bloop
responses.allies: Bloop
responses.coterie: Bloop
responses.enemies: Bloop
responses.opposed_covenant: Bloop
responses.intolerable_behaviours: Bloop

boons_debts.description: Bloop

responses.court_motivation: Conrad is an inquisitor, meaning his foremost duty is to assess his peers' dedication to the scriptures of Longinus and to directly enact the Bishop's will. The obligations of his station and loyalty to his covenant are what bring him to Court.
responses.ambitions_sydney: Conrad wishes to see the Lancea et Sanctum rule Sydney, induct more nameless kindred into its ranks, and severely diminish the powers of the Circle witches. He also hopes to attain the rank of Sheriff so that he might have more power in his inquisitorial role.
responses.why_sydney: Conrad was given a holy mission by his superiors to bring the word of Longinus to the new frontier in Australia.
responses.why_covenant: Conrad was converted after the Embrace, seeing no other explanation for the Damned condition other than the intervention of a wrathful, vengeful God serving him just desserts for a lifetime of sinfulness. He was recruited by his own Sire.
responses.covenant_goals: As an inquisitor, Conrad is expected to oversee his peers and ensure they are all pursuing the goal of spreading Longinus's message in a proper manner. He sees himself as the Bishop's right hand and the one with the most unwavering faith in the word of Longinus.
responses.clan_goals: Conrad is incredibly conservative in his adherence to the Book of Longinus and is incredibly intolerant of those who would stray from its teachings, especially within his own covenant. His goal to maintain ideological purity within his own group and to extinguish any and all blasphemy.
responses.aspired_position: Enforcer (Sheriff or Inquisitor General)
responses.view_traditions: The traditions are the three commandments are to never be broken. Conrad respects the traditions and would see them enforced to the letter.
responses.view_elysium: Conrad enjoys the civility that Elysium engenders. His work is achieved primarily through friendly discussion and covert interrogation, so Elysium aligns with his methods and goals. Conrad has little aptitude for violence or intimidation so it suits him well.
responses.view_mortals: The Kine are God's flock and must be treated as such. When they stray from the path they must be scared back toward it and when they are under attack they must be shielded. Conrad sees himself as a sheep dog, accepting his superiority over humans and ghouls but treating them with the same fondness one might afford to a pet. Conrad is invested in the moral and spiritual health of humanity at large.
responses.embrace_story: Conrad was on the brink of death after confronting the man that would be his Sire, having been critically injured and close to death. His Sire heard voices telling him to embrace the police detective and interpreted them as a sign from God. He resisted serving penance for this act, as he was certain it was the Holy Ghost that had demanded Conrad be cursed.
responses.sire_story: Dr Thomas Flanders (active) - Conrad's mentor who made certain that he received a thorough and comprehensive education, meted with great discipline and hardship. He worked as a forensic pathologist and mortician alongside Conrad before embracing him.
responses.early_nights: London
responses.last_city_politics: London is a typical, traditional Kindred city ruled primarily by the stuffy elite Invictus, seconded by the Sanctified.
responses.touchstones: Conrad's granddaughter moved to Sydney some years ago (a significant reason as to why he was so keen to leave London) and she greatly resembles Conrad's late wife
responses.hunting_style_note: Conrad stalks and ambushes his prey
responses.common_indulgences: Conrad enjoys the theatre, especially musicals, and is able to enjoy them freely as most productions are staged at night.
responses.allies:
Carver (Archer W) - a former Crone turned antiquarian after meddling with powers beyond their comprehension. Conrad was involved in the apprehension of this dangerous individual and oversaw their redemption when they sought to abandon the Circle for the Lance. Conrad likes Carver because they represent the fact that any Kindred can find purpose with the Lance and not all are truly lost.

Kirk Grimm (Phil G) - a member of the Crimson Cavalry who turned traitor on their Sire to help the rest of the Lance expel them from London. Conrad and Kirk have worked together ever since, using their complementary brains and brawn to solve mysteries, including a string of werewolf attacks that were falsely attributed to Kirk. They are a professional duo and were sent to Australia together because of their teamwork.

Name Currently Unknown (Alana W) -  Conrad heard tales of their Sire, a colonial relic living in India, and sympathised with the unfortunate circumstances of their Embrace and improper education into the Lance. Conrad feels a need to rectify the abuse this kindred suffered at the hands of their parent.
responses.coterie: Conrad is in a coterie with Kirk Grimm
responses.enemies:
Charlie Ballsack (Kurtis C) - Charlie and Conrad have had several run-ins which have all ended poorly. There was an incident where he kidnapped a kindred targeted for inquistorial questioning, the time he slew one of Conrad's friends who had been set up as bait by hunters, and the bounty he collected on a man who had used the Lance by faking repentance to escape criminal persecution in another city. Conrad knows that Charlie will do whatever pays him the most and dislikes his lack of principles.

Keeper / Mnemosyne (Symon G) - Conrad's grandsire and secret stalker. Upon learning he had a grandchilde, Keeper began eating the memories of Conrad from his wife's mind, lobotomising her and stealing all she remembered of her husband. Conrad suspects him to have done this but has not had an opportunity to act on those suspicions. The relationship is outwardly friendly, but Conrad harbours deep resentment for his terrifying progenitor's progenitor.

Rene (Luca V) - a French upstart from the Carthian Movement. She and Conrad have always been at each another's throats, using protest movements and the police in a proxy war. Their squabbles reached a tipping point when St Leon's, a church in London, burnt down during a riot, killing some Kindred housed in the basement, and both blamed the other for the incident.
responses.opposed_covenant: Conrad despises the Circle of the Crone as they are servants of the Devil and slaves to their Beast. He also thoroughly dislikes the Carthian Movement as he had an exceptionally privileged upbringing and dislikes people who would bring about changes to the status quo.
responses.intolerable_behaviours: Conrad dislikes blasphemy and taking the Lord's name in vain. He has very little tolerance for any violation of the three traditions and prides himself on a spotless record.

mortal_family.description: Conrad has a living granddaughter

responses.gaming_style_note: I'm looking forward to playing a character whose path has been smoothed by their (off-screen but utterly terrifying) sire for decades as they move from place to place as their emissary. Suddenly Cyrus is alone, potentially surrounded by Kindred who think _he's_ the reason they were robbed, banished, attacked, left for dead, socially ostracised, or all of the above. How does the bon vivant who's lead a charmed unlife fare when all he has to fall back on is how well he were paying attention for all those years?
responses.support_note: I'll need support making sure that the PvP I bring stings people but doesn't destroy them - at least not in the first encounter. I've spent several years playing a "You won't know until the knife is in" character that operates largely in PvE contexts with most PvP being "stay out of my way until this is done / help me get this done or you'll suffer".
responses.court_motivation: Cyrus' sire (Helena, never onscreen) expects Cyrus to keep her abreast of what is happening in Sydney as the various clans and covenants jockey for status. She will be _very_ upset if he's not there to ensure she knows what's happening...and making sure that her specific desires for the city are being woven into broader Daeva and Crone plans...
responses.ambitions_sydney: Helena has made it clear to Cyrus that he needs to create a lovely corner of the city for any fellows Crones who wish to come and stay. One place is good, one place and several additional bolt-holes is infinitely preferable. Establish a beachhead - one with decent views and ready access to the kine, darling.
responses.why_sydney: Cyrus has gone where he's told for nearly 30 years. This, however, looks like a long-term assignment - something completely out of step with what he's used to in his role as traveling messenger/ambassador/emissary. It's...unsettling.
responses.why_covenant: Helena saw that a practitioner of cult-like musical gatherings engaged in drug-fueled ecstatic dance was a natural for the worship of the Crone. Cyrus' barely-remembered BA in Ancient History gave him just enough understanding of Dionysian rituals to create the necessary grounding and framework.
responses.covenant_goals: Cyrus believes that the Crones have a marketing problem. There's a thousand ways to connect with the Goddess, and why go the hard sell with shadowy gathering and complicated rituals if he can achieve the same thing with a well-timed bassline drop?
responses.clan_goals: Daeva are too narrow in how they approach the living. DJing is the art of creating a moment shared with many others. Something fleeting, crafted with sound and light and shared energy. What can be more seductive that giving the kine a moment like that and promising another...and another...and another...
responses.aspired_position: Socialite
responses.view_traditions: The Masquerade is easier to keep than elders think. So many things a mortal _thought_ they saw can be blamed on lighting, drugs and alcohol, the vibe. Hunt not where you are unseen but where people won't believe what they've seen. Progeny should be given the benefit of the doubt but have sire and childe punished equally for the whelp's sins. Amaranth is untenable.
responses.view_elysium: Very much so.
responses.view_mortals:
Cyrus is fond of humans. They're an audience that will give him honest, unfiltered, moment-to-moment feedback on what he creates when he's performing. The vibe doesn't lie.

Cyrus has two ghouls who act as his tech team and (unbeknownst to him) occasional babysitters for when his hedonism gets in the way of Helena's plans. Like everyone else, he reminds them of a Golden Retriever. Unlike everyone else, they know where the lead and treats are.
responses.embrace_story: A ritual of mutual blood-letting held within Helena's coven.
responses.sire_story: Helena De Witt (imagine if they'd cast Helena Bonham Carter to play Lucifer in the TV series but given her exactly the same acting notes). She saw Cyrus as an ideal means to keep her global network of favours, deals, double-crosses, plans, and the odd violent takeover ticking along. Both the man himself and the events he performs at provide cover for a myriad of moves to be made.
responses.early_nights: Cyrus was embraced in Los Angeles, he spent his first few years of unlife on the West Coast before some national outings as Helena's messenger boy. She kept her lad on a short leash.
responses.last_city_politics: The last city Cyrus spent more than a month in was London, where a cabal of Ventrue Invictus rule from the financial district. He kept to lifestyle districts like Soho and was left more or less alone - he thinks. There was at least one attempt on his unlife by an old enemy of Helena's that was foiled by one of the ghouls.
responses.touchstones: The music of his parents, particularly the Beatles.
responses.hunting_style_note: Feeding at a club event, festival or rave is trivial. Do you have any idea what people will do to get backstage, or how carried away they can get once they're there?
responses.first_kill: A full-moon party in Thailand. Had to throw the body into the ocean. It turned out she was underage. Parents were in the news for weeks. Never again.
responses.common_indulgences: Cyrus is a sucker for any big crowd enjoying itself.
responses.allies:
Brandy (CoC) - She's asked him to make sure her ladies from the DDs get an occasional Weekend They'll Never Forget at huge events like EDC,  she's made sure permits for his events fly through city planning meetings like they have wings.

Einar Solvieg (Carthian) - These two have seen each other on at least three different continents at this point. Nothing more than mutual recognition and The Nod, but game recognise game, and if something goes pear-shaped they'll go back-to-back.
responses.coterie: Not as yet (other than the coven with other CoC players)
responses.enemies: I am SO hoping so. Cyrus has been a harbinger for any number of vampires (he even knew about it a few times), and having a few people at court holding him responsible for things going awry would be outstanding.
responses.opposed_covenant: Fuck the Invictus. Scheming gits have eternity and they treat it like a profit and loss statement.
responses.intolerable_behaviours: Treating humans as cattle.

mortal_family.description: Helena chose carefully - Cyrus had older parents living in middle America who were dead by the time she finally let him leave LA. Cyrus donates to their favorite charities without her knowledge.

boons_debts.description: Brandi and I probably owe each other at least 1-2 boons apiece.

responses.gaming_style_note: I am still working this out but I think I enjoy direct confrontation. I enjoy personal drama woven into schemes.
responses.support_note: Managing complex rules, direct guidance to ensure rules are followed.
responses.court_motivation: Back when Etsy was a "Made guy" the quiet ones got nothing, no one looked for them when they disappeared, and thats not power. That's doom. The court is not just where the power is, thats where the "real" vampires are seen, its where the notorious play their tune.
responses.ambitions_sydney: Word has it Sydney is ripe for take over, a city free from the Kindred, just asking for us to return. Etsy wants in, he hopes to climb the ladder and be part of the conversation...any conversation. To be the guy, you know, the one who gets it done, the one you ask, the guy who gives you a good time or your enemy a bad time...that guy. Attention and validation.
responses.why_sydney: On the surface its about pursuing opportunity but this isn't his first court. He attended some in New York...and insulted the wrong Lords. He ain't a guy to hide, pretend he's Joey Nobody. That ain't Etsy. Sydney...there's something.
responses.touchstones: His Mother. His childhood home in the Bronx. His mothers grave.
responses.allies: Baron Rene (despite the caning he got from him, he is an invictus baron), Jack Fallow (uneasy but Jack reached out to offer "help")
responses.coterie: A group of other invictus members.
responses.enemies: Half the court after the first attendance?
responses.opposed_covenant: The Circle of the Crone.

responses.gaming_style_note:
I enjoy personal horror alongside political schemes, am looking forward to improving in my subtle manipulation roleplaying skills, and would like to try being a major political player to become more confident in my RP as well.

I feel I would leave sessions feeling satisfied if I can come to a place where I am more confident playing a character that I don't feel reflects a part of my own personality.
responses.support_note: I would LOVE help developing political schemes, and would love guidance where I already know that I struggle in social pvp games (lying convincingly, and telling the truth in a way that doesn't seem suspicious either)
responses.court_motivation: Edna is seeking power for power's sake. Watching carefully set pieces fall in to place makes her feel a similar rush that a lesser being might feel when an arm wrestling match begins to turn in their favour. Simply, she is mad enough that she feels court is a regular way to get out and keep her skills sharp.
responses.ambitions_sydney: Sydney is a long term plan for Edna. She plans to establish territory and welcome as many souls in to her flock as she can.
responses.why_sydney: She has dreams of having a second try of 1970s American Evangelism, and doing it the right way this time. She may not have a face for a movement, but she views Sydney as belonging to her by right of God
responses.why_covenant: Edna was previously Mormon, before embrace. The claim from her sire was that her Mormonism was heresy that she must pay penance for. She was initially recruited to be a sort of vampiric sin-eater (in the ritual sense) for the church to pay this penance
responses.covenant_goals: Outwardly, Edna wishes to be seen as an intellectual and a caregiver. One worthy of respect both for her ability to proselytize and serve the Flock humbly. Inwardly, she wishes for the church to unwittingly assist her (as she is herself hideous and deeply unpersonable) in increasing her own power and restoring the city to the way God intended it to be.
responses.clan_goals: Edna has learned to embrace living as a Nosferatu. Love and adoration do not lend itself to control as efficiently as fear.
responses.aspired_position: Bishop
responses.view_traditions:
Masquerade: Necessary
Progeny: Resentful of. While understanding it is necessary for most, she knows that she should be allowed to do so, as she can see who God needs to grow his army of the damned
Amaranth: Views it as outdated, but has no desire for it on her own.
responses.view_elysium: Only when watched. It is a civilised necessity, she only desires to break Elysium when she believes in would not be unbecoming of her to do so. Use of Nightmare just to get your way with a lesser being is lazy, after all.
responses.view_mortals: Mortals are food primarily. But they are useful when they worship her? HIM. When they worship Him. Our Lord. Her hobby is maintaining a herd for her Flock to feed from, as she doesn't get out much.
responses.embrace_story: Edna was attacked while accompanying her late husband in Ohio while he was Mission President. It was a violent embrace, of which her husband did not complete with her.
responses.sire_story: Gwylim Lloyd (destroyed) embraced Edna to be eternal companion to her husband, who Lloyd viewed to be a fitting candidate for a new Paladin to the church, her husband did not make it through the change. Edna felt free when her Sire was destroyed. He got cocky. She knew there was so much more to be done outside of his sphere of influence. She sees no need to mention him outside of formal requirements. He was an embarrassment to himself.
responses.early_nights: They were embraced in Cincinnati Ohio, and spent her early nights performing ritualistic sin-eating for her sire and fellow kindred of the cloth beneath the grounds at Old St. Mary's Church. Her needs for survival were tended to by a kind Kindred Sister of the church, but as she was not allowed to leave the grounds, she began to lose her grip on her sanity.
responses.last_city_politics: NOLA was and still is under Carthian Rule. Currently unstable but also hotly contested by the Lance and Invictus.
responses.touchstones:
Edna has moved her husbands remains with her everywhere she has moved to. She visit and tends to it regularly as the seasons change, on his birthday, and on the date of their wedding anniversary. It challenges her vampiric nature as she struggles to square up the woman that he chose to marry under Heavenly Father with the woman she has become. She also struggles in a way because she is glad he is dead. Because she would not want him to see her as this changed beast who does not truly miss the woman she was at all. But she likes to chat to him, and try to pretend for him all the same. As she should, devoted wife she always was.

Her own copy of The Book of Mormon challenges her vampiric nature as she came in to the Lancea Sanctum being punished for her faith in Heavenly Father. Her foundations as a member of the Lance was one that was designed to shake her belief, which she has never healed, although that is something partly secret even to herself.
responses.hunting_style_note: Edna cultivates willing vessels (cult). While she treats them as livestock, they are well loved livestock. Just as Sister Agnes kindly maintained her in Ohio, she maintains her herd. She feeds them fed well and watered, gives them her sermons, washes them and keeps their hair and nails neat and tidy. She keeps their clothes clean and changes their chamber pots daily. Healthy herd, healthy flock. She loves them, and they love her.
responses.first_kill: When Edna found herself free from her master, she had nobody to guide her on how exactly to be a vampire. Her faith broken, feeling as if merely a vessel full of the sins of others, she wanted for the first time for a sin in her heart to be her own. To test God's will and see if he was truly there. She wanted to let it happen, but couldn't bring herself to know the answer.
responses.common_indulgences: Her secretly broken faith emerges in her obsessive study of theology. The Testament of Longinus is the only book that has shown her the truth of God. She wishes to understand his truth more, so she might be a better servant to Him. She reads all abrahamic scriptures canon and apocryphal and the theological studies surrounding them. She has done well in her years so far to ignore the sense in the back of her mind that God does not exist except within her. That would be heresy.
responses.allies: Edna is not very personable. She had a relatively non-confrontational working relationship with Kirk Grimm (to be confirmed) during the rise of evangelism in the United States in the 1970s where they both played their roles well
responses.coterie: No
responses.enemies: No
responses.opposed_covenant: Carthians in particular stir feelings of disgust in Edna. To her they are all pretense. Vampires cannot maintain control enough to avoid hunters in the company of anarchists and their sympathisers.
responses.intolerable_behaviours: Edna does not tolerate people being framed to pay penance for others sins. Sinners should face god as they are and throw themselves at his mercy. She can only be convinced of letting this happen for the good of the Church, but it is difficult for her to not be noticeably bothered by it.

mortal_family.description: Edna has one descendent she watches while she was staying in NOLA: her great, great niece  Ellen. Mostly out of pure fascination for how she lives, having disaffiliated with the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter Day Saints, divorced her husband, and is entering her 60s drinking wine and eating beignets with her chattering little friends.

boons_debts.description: To be determined

responses.character_name: Einar Solveig (Which translates to "One Warrior on the Path of the Sun", which seems uniquely problematic for a Kindred.
responses.blood_potency:
BP 2

Despite being quite old, Einar has spent a large chunk of time in torpor - locked in a chest at the bottom of the ocean. Not exactly the kind of pirate treasure the persons who found him were looking for.
responses.gaming_style_note:
I am planning to play things by ear a bit with this chron. I am not opposed to developing the character into a major political player, but I am intending to be a bit more of a wild-card and develop my own objectives as the character grows.

I enjoy direct confrontation and would be more than happy to throw down at the drop of a hat, but I also thrive among subtle manipulations and long term shenanigans.
responses.support_note: I am pretty confident with the world, rules, and political machinations of the game, so beyond providing rules clarifications and oversight I am unsure at this stage what I personally need from STs.
responses.bloodline_rationale: This Bloodline appealed to me originally as I prefer to physically look like that character I am playing, and having grown my beard out for almost a year I wanted to lean into that reality, and make a character that it makes sense to have a beard for. Playing a Kindred Viking certainly fits that concept, and is an idea I can really sink my teeth into as far as characterization goes. The Rotgrafen bloodline has a history that I find engaging, and offers access to multiple powers in clan that I was hoping to be able to play around with this chron.
responses.court_motivation: Einar is an older kindred who has spent little time in civilised Kindred society. He has few allies, and not much in the way of reputation or deeds of renown to point to for cudos and respect. Arriving in Sydney, virgin territory as it is, Einar hopes to make a name for himself, entering into a new land, a new environment, helping to build a new Kindred society from scratch - putting himself on a level playing field with those who make their way to this new world.
responses.ambitions_sydney: Einar hopes to establish a territory for himself, make a name, and undermine that plans of the Lancea Sanctum wherever he can.
responses.why_sydney:
Einar saw opportunity to further himself and gain power in a way that he might never be able to in a more established city.

Though he was already headed to Sydney to represent the Carthian Movement, he has been tasked with the support and protection of a younger Kindred as a result of an indiscretion in London.
responses.why_covenant: Einar sought out membership among the Carthians after realising that he had little to offer by way of reputation and resources despite his advance years, and would therefore never make it in the Invictus.
responses.covenant_goals: Einar does secretly desire power, as Ventrue are want to do, and will take whatever opportunities he can to elevate himself within the Carthians. Einar feels that if he cannot take advantage of his age among the Invictus, approaching the Carthians and suing for a more level playing field among a group that values "equality" puts him in a much better position to attain the power and respect that he deserves.
responses.clan_goals:
As a Rotrgrafen, Einar is extremely different to the typical Venture physically, mentally, and philosophically. Understanding how dangerous Ventrue are, he is more interested in attempting to mitigate the danger he may face from them.

Einar sees the Ventrue as potentially the most dangerous Kindred in the world. Having domain over the mind - manipulating a person's decision making processes, controlling their actions, and even altering their memories is quite simply offensive to an independent person who values their individuality.
responses.aspired_position: Einar is a Viking Mercenary who far more leans towards the Enforcer side of things.
responses.view_traditions:
Einar was an unsanctioned Embrace. He finds the idea of having to seek permission to Sire to be a judicial overreach.

The Masquerade is important, but Einar has had to unlearn the Rotgrafen attitude of "leave no witnesses alive", regardless of how effective it is, which is deemed less than ideal in modern nights.

Amaranth would generally be frowned upon - but consuming the soul of a powerful opponent after defeating them in battle would not be too different to his ancestors consuming the heart of a fallen enemy to gain their strength.
responses.view_elysium: Einar would very much find Elysium restrictive and unnecessary.
responses.view_mortals: Einar has a fondness for certain Ghouls after one saved his un-life a few decades ago, but in general he sees Kine as little more than cattle.
responses.embrace_story:
Einar was a sailor for the Dutch East India Company when in the mid 1840's, his ship was attacked by pirates. Leading a valiant, but futile defense - the ship was overrun, and Einar was taken captive along with several other survivors.

Having shown courage and skill in combat, the Captain of the Pirates made Einar an offer - join them in an eternal undeath of violence, chaos, and adventure - or have his throat cut, his blood drained, and be thrown to the fish.
responses.sire_story:
Captain Olga Andersson (Destroyed)

Captain Andersson was the leader of the Rotgrafen crew who capture Einar and his ship. She sired Einar after watching him lead the defense of the Merchant Navy vessel on which he served. She mentored him in the ways of the Kindred, teaching him to fight, feed, and to tap into the powers of the blood.
responses.early_nights: Einar was embraced in international waters and spent his early requiem as a Pirate operating around the Carribean Sea. Einar had little to no contact with Kindred society in general for the first several of decades of his un-life and learned most of what he knows from the Pirates and Mercenaries that he called "family".
responses.last_city_politics:
Einar has been something of a nomad for most of his Requiem - spending a lot of time at sea, traversing countries and continents, never really spending enough time in one place to put down roots.

Einar's last city prior to coming to Sydney was London, where he ran afoul of one of the dominant Invictus Old Bloods who run the city.
responses.touchstones:
Einar spends a portion of his downtime working on the construction of a wooden sail-boat. It is not really about the boat itself (though it is truly his heart's desire to one day to take it out on the water and sail a vessel that he has made with his own hands), it is about the craft, the process, and the artistry - creating something that reminds him of his life before, and speaks to his bloodline's inherent connection to the sea.

Einar also has a drinking buddy named Kyle. Everywhere he goes, Einar seeks out another Kyle - a regular at one of the drinking holes near the docks. Einar will maintain and protect his Kyle, meeting up with him about once a month for a therapeutic booze-up. While Kyle gets trashed, Einar will talk through his problems while also genuinely learning about Kyle's. Kyle acts as a link to Einar's past, a sounding board, and vicarious alcoholic.
responses.hunting_style_note: Einar prefers to feed on Kine who are under the influence of one substance or another. He likes to hang around bars, taking advantage of those who are too intoxicated to fight back or care, as well as purposefully initiating fights with those who seem like they are spoiling for one so that he can put them on their ass, and feed on the wonderful cocktail of alcohol and adrenaline that pumps through their arteries.
responses.first_kill: After being turned, Einar was locked in the brig of his Sire's ship along with several of his surviving comrades. Unable to control himself in the newborn hunger - he was left to consume and kill all of them before he was even permitted to leave and join his new crew.
responses.common_indulgences: Einar enjoys working on his ship, drinks alcohol even if it does nothing for him, and is an avid fighter. He would have a heavy bag set up in his haven that he spends a lot of time training on, and never baulks at the opportunity to spar, or to fight.
responses.allies:
Doc and MacHeath (Jess and Charlie) - Carthian's from the UK. Einar knows these two from his time in London, and sees that they are an asset, and as trustworthy a pair of Kindred as he is likely to find and possessed of skills that will be invaluable to the Movement.

Cyrus (Bill) - Einar and Cyrus have run into each other a number of times over several decades and in a number of different countries, usually at raves and music festivals. Party animals who just seem to "get" each other - the pair have helped each other out of, and into a whole bunch of trouble. There are more than a dozen bodies buried in a number of cities that only the two of them know the locations of.

Eve (Jamie) - The Childe of a powerful Invictus Elder who manufactured a situation where Einar would be beholden to them, owing them several minor boons. They have demanded that Einar travel to Australia with Eve and operate as their young Childe's protector until such time as their investment in Sydney is secure.
