responses.coterie: Ren (Luca Venn) - a fellow Carthian who Einar connected with in London prior to travelling to Australia. The Cell who sent them to Sydney has set them up in a shared lodging so that Einar can protect Ren, and Ren can make sure Einar doesn't get himself into too much trouble.
responses.enemies: I have not yet established any enemies among the players base, but I am sure it will not take long!
responses.opposed_covenant: Due to the history of his Bloodline, Einar is fundamentally opposed to the Lancea Sanctum. He will do whatever he can to ensure they do not find out what he is and will make every effort to undermine their goals wherever he can.
responses.intolerable_behaviours:
Einar is a free spirit, valuing his personal autonomy above all - particularly the sanctity of his mind and memories. If you don't have control over your actions, and can't trust your own memory, you have nothing. You ARE nothing. This is why he is not a massive fan of the Ventrue, despite sharing blood. They are far too dangerous.

Einar does not like being spoken down to. Despite his age, his un-life spent as a Pirate, Mercenary, and Nomad has meant that he is more or less unknown no matter where he goes. For some Kindred, the lack of familiarity means that he is a nobody - which is why despite being a 183 year old Ventrue, he was never able to find a home in the Invictus, and has fallen in with the Carthians instead.

mortal_family.description: Einar is too old to have living family, at least non whom he would have known while he was alive.

boons_debts.description: Einar owes three minor boons to Eve (Jamie) by way of their Sire.
Einar owes three minor boons to Eve (Jamie) by way of their Sire.

responses.high_concept: Multi-Millionare CEO but "one of the good ones"
responses.gaming_style_note: Political Chess except every third piece has a mind of its own. I love games where despite the best efforts of powerful characters shit goes sideways, forcing individuals/covenants to act and think on the fly. e.g. In the previous chronicle during the Hector debacle the chaos that followed with every covenant trying to one up or undermine the other while also maintain the masquerade. While much of the wheeling and dealing goes on behind (each characters) scenes moments where it's really brought to the surface are amazing
responses.support_note: I think going into this chronicle no longer as the brand new player, feedback on how best to support new players as moments arise would be very helpful. Knowing when to let a new player make the mistake or when to intervene for example. Many of these moments are situation dependant but being able to check in about them would be great
responses.court_motivation: The Sydney court is where Eve belongs, the twins have been preparing for sydney they're entire immortal life. Their sire has entrenched court politics, tactics, niceties into their brain through years of tests and trials. To their sire, Sydney is a new start and a second chance at taking Australia, to Eve, it's the only chance they're ever going to get at building something to stand against him. Court is how Eve keeps their investment safe, ensures Sydney thrives and by proxy themselves.
responses.ambitions_sydney: Eve hopes to build their company (and it's cult underbelly) away from the grasping tendrils of their sire. Sydney offers a haven from his reach and there first chance to spread their wings without facing his ire. It also is the perfect staging ground for her silent rebellion, Sydney must become a Carthian stronghold, it must become their stronghold. They must be ready, for eventually the vale will fade and he will arrive.
responses.why_sydney: Eve and their sister were specifically sired with the intent of taking Australia for their sire. He was part of the first attempt to colonise Australia by the Dutch and after the aftermath sees this as the perfect opportunity to try again.
responses.why_covenant: Eve was originally a member of the Invictus, by birth right not by choice. Over the years of working for their sire, pulling favours and making gentle moves across Europe they started to realise their own beliefs where at odds with that of the Invictus. Eve was quite often "on the ground" ensuring company expansion went smoothly or helping acquire useful assets thi opened her eyes to the disconnect between her sire and his ilk and reality. It was shortly after this that she met Rene, a slightly older Carthian kindred who's slightly radical and distant nature easily convinced Eve that their heart lay with the Carthian movement.
responses.covenant_goals: Eve understands her standing with the Carthians is thin, having only been an Invictus member in the last decade, they know they've got their work cut out for them. They must be gentle, they are very aware of how much she needs the movement, she now only needs convince the others. They must balance their influence with respect, they cannot be a simple pawn for the movement but push to hard and they'll be disregarded as a fake.
responses.clan_goals: Eve thinks little of her bloodline, but that may be purely due to a lack of self awareness. They're a Daeva and they'll be damned if anyone doesn't immediately recognise that, their looks and their charm have been a weapon since before they were kindred,
responses.aspired_position: Ruler or Primogen,
responses.view_traditions: The uphold the Masquerade and respect it above all and look down at those that would tempt fate with breaking it. They understand that Progeny exists, but they also see the duality of it. Progeny is only law to those not powerful enough to ignore it, their sire barely asked permission before their creation, they've heard of elders who do so as well. But if they were to do it, well for them it's a law that must be respected... for now.
responses.view_elysium: Yes, Elysium is essential to peace and negotiating, which in the end is vital for business. Business cannot be done if kindred are fight and wailing over whoever called the other a cur. Elysium enforces the checking of beast (and ego) at the door.
responses.view_mortals: They pity ghouls, they logically understand why some kindred may choose to ghoul a mortal but they see it as cheating, the easy way out. Keeping mortals happy, secluded, content and churning along is what Eve has always done best. She sees mortals as having value, especially those within her company. Every single mortal within her company is under her protection, they are the source of her influence, they are her influence and power. Any mortal that would threaten them is seen as lesser and any kindred who would is a threat.
responses.embrace_story: The Lockridge Family was a globally known small family of elites that operated a logistics firm for decades, that was until their private plane crashed, they only two not on the plane where Eve and Arden Lockridge. Except that their family was already long dead before they got on the plane, which piloted by a ghoul flew straight into a mountain. Eve's sire Hendrik had attempted through his influence and by extending his cult to infiltrate Lockridge Global Logistics, however when the family showed a slight resistance to his influence this enraged him. Arden and Eve's memory of their embrace is hazy, they remember watching their family die at the hands of their sire, they remember watching the new story of the plan crash. But for weeks... maybe months they were in limbo, being groomed and trained by their sire.
responses.sire_story: Hendrik van der maas, active Invictus Elder. (previously mentioned reasoning for embrace), Hendrik is an extremely influential Invictus elder from Denmark who seeks to use Eve and their twin Arden to spread his tendrils of influence and control to Australia. Arden is the golden twin, always receiving Hendriks praise and boons while Eve has a more troublesome relationship. Hendrik has always enjoyed pushing Eve, seeing how much they can take of their torment and torture and just when they're about to snap, they real them back in. This has lessened over the last half a decade as it appears to have lost some of its fun to Hendrik.
responses.early_nights: Copenhagen for a time, however their sire ensured that in the first decade of their "training" they experienced many different courts. Only ever ones were his influence could be felt but felt none the less.
responses.last_city_politics: Same as above, Eve has experienced many different courts, never in a long term sense but often having to show face, pay respects, shake hands before getting down to business in a new city. They're prepared enough for Sydney in that they know to be prepared for anything, this is a new world far away from other kindred, Anything could go.
responses.touchstones:
Bill Manchester was a deputy site manager at an LGL shipping dock in the late nighties. Eve and Arden had been raised to take over this company long before their knowledge of Kindred, part of that involved working in varying different aspects of the company often away from family and connection. Eve worked under Bill for almost a year, while at first starting as a manager, then a friend before becoming a much ended mentor. He was their when Eve secured her first shipping contract, taught her the secrets of managing people, picked her up from a date gone wrong and woke her up for work with a coffee after a big night. When eve moved back home they stayed in contact, until they were turned. 

Even after turning Bill kept in contact, checking in, he sends flowers every year on the fake anniversary of their parents death. Eve has kept in contact but also kept their distance as best they could, terrified that their sire might influence or seek to destroy him. A few months before moving to Sydney, Eve happened upon plans in London to expand Hadriks Cult of Influence to hold a more secure foothold of the docks. Upon hearing this Eve knew they had to get Bill out, and so they pulled a few strings and offered him more money then he could say no to. A new business venture, Assests manager to the joint-CEO in the Australiasia pacific region. Of course he said yes, and Eve could breath easier, for now.
responses.hunting_style_note: Eve feeds in two ways: For fun and for function. The latter is often from members of her sires cult that have formed into her herd. It's a rather joyless feed but efficient and safe. However if Eve is blessed with extra time they love to indulge in bars and ngihtclubs, attracting lovers and leaving a trail of heartache and headaches behind them.
responses.first_kill: A couple years before moving to Sydney Eve had failed to acquire shipping rights to an almost essential lane allowing traversal past Spain. The panic of what their sire may do and the anger, frustration was pent up within when they decided to blow of some steam. They hit the clubs, they had fed of many mortals under the influence of drugs by the time they chose their suiter. They were taken back to a modest apartment where in the blur and the heat of the moment, for just a second they lost control. Seconds later they'd pulled back, the man alive, but seconds from being otherwise. Eve panicked and left, their beast silently screaming for more.
responses.common_indulgences: Drugs through others blood, nightlight, clubbing, seduction. They still follow a self care ritual despite it being all but pointless and enjoy a smoke even though it doesn't calm the nerves anymore.
responses.allies:
Rene: The kindred that helped flip eve to the carthian movement, they share a somewhat friendship, with Eve occasionally turnign to them for guidance on how to handle situations in "the carthian way". They have offered an olive branch to Rene and brought them to Sydney ( regardless if they planned already to come)

Einar: A very recent addition to Eve's allies, after being snookered into protecting Eve by their Sire Einer is now obligated to protect them.. for a bit. Eve has made it clear that they are not cut from the same cloth and hope that while not exactly a great start, that it might be a mutually beneficial relationship
responses.coterie: No
responses.opposed_covenant: Invictus, in undermining the invictus it undermines Eve's sire. If they were to thrive in Sydney then come a few decades there would be nothing standing between Eve's destruction.

mortal_family.description: As mentioned above, Family dead dead

boons_debts.description: Rene: Minor Boon

responses.high_concept: Lady Godiva Trash Panda; unknown patron saint of the unwanted, kept by the dark, never cherished.
responses.apparent_age: She could pass for anywhere from 20s - 30s. Potentially even into 40s if she's feeling particularly exhausted or haggard.
responses.gaming_style_note: I am a subtle manipulation kinda person. But, as discussed with y'all, I'm more interested in supporting others and playing to lose, for myself, but through that playing to lift the experience of others.
responses.support_note: Will continue to engage with Kurtis and Symon re: play to lose :)
responses.court_motivation:
Ivana attends Court, as she understands it right now, primarily because Charlie does. She believes she owes him a life debt for saving her from destruction, and she does not feel safe being absent from the places where his risks and obligations gather. Court is dangerous, but distance feels more dangerous still. Staying visible near him - and near power - feels like the most practical way to remain useful and difficult to discard.

She also believes Court may be the best place available to learn how to be what she has become. She studies people, behaviour, hierarchy, and belonging by watching others and adjusting herself in response. Court gives her somewhere to observe and imitate, even if she never fully feels she belongs there. Whether that instinct leads to growth or to trouble is something she has not yet discovered. It will almost certainly lead to questionable fashion decisions - she is far more comfortable without clothing, and only wears it because she was advised it improves social outcomes in certain situations, such as Court - so she tends to copy whatever the nearest socially successful predator appears to be wearing. Given that the definition of 'successful' is chosen by Ivana, the results are often mixed, questionable and occasionally outright alarming.

She wants to help where she can, and Court is where needs, conflicts, and bargains surface. If she is going to matter, she thinks it will be because she shows up when things are at stake. 

There is also a faint pull she cannot fully explain - a half-remembered prophecy about stars and fate from someone she is almost certain she encountered in the real world, when she was human. She does not trust the memory, but she feels drawn to Court all the same. She assumes this is coincidence or nerves. It may not be.

All of these reasons are true for her now - but they are beliefs, not certainties, and they may change as she changes. Or they may not. Who knows? Once play starts, Ivana will make her own decisions, and to hell what Lyn wants!
responses.ambitions_sydney:
Ivana?s current goal in Sydney is simple and immediate: survive, and remain close to Charlie. He is the reason she exists, and staying near him feels like the only way to make sense of her life - and the only way to avoid being discarded entirely. Her sense of purpose, such as it is, revolves around him.

Beyond that, she suspects that Sydney might offer something she has never had: a chance to figure out who she is when she is not simply a pawn in someone else?s schemes. She does not think in terms of power or status, but she wants to belong, to be useful, and - quietly, almost fearfully - to begin claiming an identity that is hers, not imposed or borrowed. Whether she will ever feel she deserves that, or has the courage to pursue it, is something even she cannot say.

If it works out, Sydney could become home - not because it is safe, but because it may be the place where she starts to become someone by her own choosing. And yet, home is still wherever Charlie is. She cannot yet imagine a version of herself that does not need him nearby; her survival and any hope of thriving are so intertwined with him that she cannot separate them... Though she suspects the story may yet change in ways she cannot predict - a thought which both frightens and intrigues her.
responses.why_sydney:
Ivana came to Sydney not because anyone truly wanted her here - not even Charlie, really - but because of things she only vaguely understands. She knows her becoming Kindred was both unauthorised and completely accidental - a posthumous embrace, which marks her as the lowest of the low. As in her human life, she remains unwanted, undesired, and just a problem. She would have been executed if Charlie hadn?t stepped in.

Though the details of the bargain Charlie struck with the Prince and Court are hazy, she knows this much: she was to go wherever Charlie went, and the Prince and Court ordered her to be ?shaped? into something vaguely worthwhile - not for her own sake, but so she wouldn?t be a liability, or worse, an embarrassment to the Court. Beyond that, the specifics are lost to her.

The bottom line, as she sees it, is that Charlie?s intervention left her here as part of a plan she was never central to. She assumes he doesn?t really want her here - after all, why would he? No one ever wanted her. Yet he has protected her before, in ways she barely understands and in other ways she is not actually aware of, and she clings to the hope that if she proves herself useful, if she can be helpful and capable, maybe he?ll keep her around. She constantly wonders whether his care and guidance are genuine, or just part of the bargain with the Prince and Court. She honestly doesn?t want to know - if she isn?t told the truth, she can keep pretending for a little while longer.

Sydney, for her, is a strange mix: exile, obligation, and tentative opportunity. It keeps her close to the one person who has chosen to protect her, while also reminding her how disposable she feels. What it will ultimately mean - home, refuge, stepping stone, or something else entirely - will become clear as events unfold.
responses.why_covenant: Ivana didn?t seek it out and wasn?t actively recruited; it just happened. When she rose from being turned, she fled to the sewers that she had been dumped in when she was murdered, and remained hidden there until the denizen's of the Court extracted her and threw her before the Prince and Court. During her time in the sewer, she stayed hidden in the dark, whispering to herself at first, and then to the darkness, begging it for help. Surprisingly, the darkness answered her, and she came to view the darkness as a guardian angel. She displayed an innate affinity for Cruac, and although she didn't pay much attention to it, or grasp the meaning of it all, others noticed - especially Charlie. Aside from her basic education at Court, as part of the bargain struck, when they moved on from Court, Charlie arranged for a contact of his, a Kindred she calls 'Scrambles' or 'Keeper' (Symon), to provide some ongoing support and mentoring. Her arrival in Sydney and attendance at Court would be the first time that she would be in a position to actively engage with her Covenant en masse, and learn more and grow.
responses.covenant_goals:
Ivana?s goals, at this point, are simple: stay useful, survive, and avoid being a liability or an embarrassment. She doesn?t aspire to climb the hierarchy, nor does she actively seek secrets or leverage, though her innate abilities give her a natural advantage in these areas. She may one day find herself doing exactly that - likely for others, not herself. Similarly, her talents could grant her subtle influence, if she ever fully realises it and chooses to act on it. It?s doubtful she will, at least in any overt way.

Any influence or leverage she does gather will probably serve one purpose above all: to prove that she is competent and helpful, and to remain just visible enough that Charlie - and perhaps, eventually, others she can learn to trust - continue to protect her, need her? and maybe, one day, even genuinely want her around. For now, survival and usefulness are her measures of success; advancement or reputation remain abstract concepts, even though her skills quietly make her far more valuable than she realises.
responses.clan_goals:
Ivana?s relationship with the essence of her Gangrel-ness is... complicated. She doesn?t aspire to rebel against traditions, nor does she aim to champion them - her focus is simply to survive, remain competent, and avoid bringing shame to herself or others.

Again, she feels as though she can't do anything right - she is a Gangrel without Protean, which is what she has come to understand is the ability that sets her Clan apart from the others. She feels that she has nothing to offer, and believes that her lack of Protean makes her weak, vulnerable and significantly less than. She expects to be sitting at the bottom of the pecking order within the Clan, and to be treated accordingly, and that it will take quite a lot for her to prove her worth amongst her Clan. 

Though she may initially appear weak or lacking at first, she doesn't understand that her bloodline and natural Crone abilities quietly set her apart, making her unusually capable in the concrete jungle, and also within social and political contexts - if she only recognise and utilise the full scope of her gifts. These abilities occasionally give her subtle leverage or influence, but she uses it usually unknowingly, and where she does use them intentionally, it is mostly to remain useful and visible enough so that she continues to be protected and useful.
responses.aspired_position: Ivana doesn't aspire to hold a role - but in the interests of picking one for this, I would say Socialiate, due to the focus on reputation the role has, and how the nuances of that tie in to her own desires.
responses.view_traditions:
Ivana understands that these are talked about as Very Important Sacred Laws That Should Never Be Broken; but has noted the inherent contradictions and hypocrisy within Kindred society that occur as a result of the Traditions being bent to serve whatever motives the Kindred in question has. Charlie's influence would also have shaped her attitude towards them.

She ultimately views them as pragmatic rules for survival, as opposed to moral imperritives - they're deadly violations that she cannot risk, because breaking them would potentially end her existence - and she's already on borrowed time, and she does not want to give anyone cause to look down upon, or hurt Charlie as a result of her actions. She follows the Traditions because it keeps her alive - and when she is alive, she has the chance to prove to Charlie and others that she is competent and useful.
responses.view_elysium:
Ivana would view Elysium as a practical structure, and her respect of it would come from pragmatism and survival instinct, as opposed to an innate or deep seated understanding of or respect for it as a structure. Similarly to the Traditions, she would likely have seen Kindred bend the rules when it suited them, and have seen the results when a Kindred has been caught - and also when they've gotten away with it. 

She would understand that getting caught breaking the rules here would draw attention and risk her survival and - more importantly - reflect poorly on Charlie. She struggles with Court in general, but particularly when Court is held at an Elysium location; because of her innate abilities and past trauma, she is acutely aware of the heavy miasma of subtle threats and social tension at every Court, which is a recipe for a violence. It is a big game, that she doesn't know the rules of and isn't allowed to stop playing. So, she tries to play the game the only way she knows how; observation, discretion and obedience. This allows her to show that she is useful, and reliable, and makes it tricky for her to make mistakes - or so she thinks - which is imperative in a world where any mistake, no matter how small, can be a death sentence.
responses.view_mortals:
Ivana has had little experience with genuine social interaction - most of her life has been as a tool, a pawn, or someone to be discarded. Her connection to humanity is limited, and she doesn?t feel much attachment to mortals in general. Mortals are practical considerations; tools, potential threats, or reminders of the fragility and despair of her former life. Ghouls, when present, are useful; servants, helpers, or sources of information. She treats them cautiously and pragmatically, prioritising survival and usefulness over sentiment.

That said, there are rare moments when her instincts break through; she has occasionally intervened to protect a young child, or someone clearly downtrodden or disadvantaged. Even then, it is quiet, careful, and pragmatic - not a moral crusade or a display of heroism. Her actions are small, subtle, and always framed by the question: ?Will this help me survive and remain useful??
responses.embrace_story:
Ivana?s Embrace was accidental, violent, and utterly unwanted by both her and the Kindred her sired her - it was an accurate mirror of her life as a human, to be honest. She was murdered by her owner (her pimp), after her last client had refused to cough up the cash because he found that her performance was 'lacking and boring' throughout the encounter to make it worth the money. The fact that her comatose state was the result of the drugs that her owner insisted on keeping her flush with, in order to keep her compliant, was irrelevant. It was her fault - she had cost him another nights earnings, and thus outlived her usefulness. 

And so, she was murdered in the back of a filthy van as it drove through a shitty and mostly disused cemetary, resigned to the fact that her death was a fitting reflection of the life she had been subjected to - her final thought was a broken prayer she barely remembered, and a thank you to death for finally claiming her.  

Her body was disposed of in a forgotten sewage shaft near a disused cemetery, meant to rot and be erased. That same night, a vicious duel for territory between two Kindred that had commenced on the streets above had spilled into her lonely resting place. It was violent and bloody, with both Kindred rending flesh from the other, seeing their precious vitae spill and mingle, seeping into the unnoticed corpse. As was customary in incidents such as this, the Court quickly engaged mortal contacts to cover up the potential Masquerade breach, which is why her corpse - assumed to be an unfortunate piece of collateral from the violent brawl - was found and processed quickly and without investigation, an inconvenience to be pushed through the system quickly and quietly, before being burried in an unmarked paupers grave in the very cemetary she had been murdered in. 

Three nights after her burial, she clawed her way back out of the grave: starved, feral, wrong. She staggered into the sewers, more animal than woman, disappearing into the darkness and sludge where she was meant to rot, terrified and alone.
responses.sire_story: Ivana was an accident - a posthumous embrace that carried the blood of the First City but no identifiable sire. Through the events of the early nights of her Requiem, she was taken on as ward and child by a Nosferatu of the Order of Sir Martin - Charlie (Kurtis). She became his obligation and Sisyphean task. Their relationship is pragmatic, cautious, and layered; he trains, protects, and mentors her, while she clings to him out of loyalty, survival, and the hope that usefulness might earn genuine care one day.
responses.early_nights:
Ivana?s first nights as a Kindred were feral, terrifying, and utterly solitary. The darkness of the sewers was both frightening and strangely comforting. She had no idea what she had become, or why she was here. She remembered, vaguely, the final moments of her human life - at least, she was fairly sure she had died - but everything that had happened since was a mystery. Her only thought was that she had clearly failed again; she couldn?t even die properly. She was a despair-filled wreck: terrified, alone, and feral.

She hid in the darkness, too afraid to move, taking refuge unknowingly near the very place her life had ended - and where her transformation had begun. Weakness and desperation overtook her, and certain smells within the sewer made her stomach rumble. She tried to locate their source, but the tunnels were too confusing, the darkness too thick, and her strength too depleted. Desperation drove her: she drank the water from the tunnels and scavenged rotting scraps, only to have her body violently reject them. Whether human or Kindred, she would likely have been sick anyway, but it offered her no insight into what had happened to her or what she had become.

In this helpless state, she whispered into the darkness, asking for help in any form it might take. Eventually, it seemed to answer, guiding her toward survival. One day, she spotted an injured rat, and instinct took over - she fed. By following these subtle cues from the shadows, she began to trust her instincts, learn from her environment, and navigate the tunnels, all while remaining hidden from the world above.

But even the darkness could not shield her from the Courts. Whispers of a ?zombie? Kindred reached the city tribunal, and Court ghouls soon dragged her into the light. Auspex probing revealed her unusual taint: the duel between two Scions of the First City had fused their vitae in her, leaving her a political and mystical anomaly with no identifiable sire. The tribunal ruled her an illegal, unstable childe and ordered her destruction.

But again, fate intervened, in the form of a Nosferatu of the Order of Sir Martin, Charlie. He came to an agreement with the Tribunal that would spare her life and see her turned into something that would not be a liability or embarrassment to the Court. And so, Charlie acquired a ward, a pathetic shadow, taking her on both as childe and as a Sisiphean task to shape into something more.
responses.last_city_politics: The city where Ivana first awoke as a Kindred was a tense place. She came to understand that the ruling Praxis was unusual; two ancient rivals had fought to a stalemate long ago, and now shared power through a tribunal. Each rival sat as one judge, while the third seat traditionally belonged to the Order of Sir Martin - a neutral party concerned only with the exact wording of compacts. She has come to the idea that this unique arrangement, coupled with Charlie's lineage within the Order, is the only reason she is alive today.
responses.touchstones: A old homeless woman named "Lady Di - the people's princess". Out with her one night, she passed out in my lap drunk, and couldn't bring myself to move her, and I bolt through the door close to sunrise nearly smoking. Charlie got grumpy, I wouldn't tell him what happened, so he followed me the next night and found my princess and then I did a bad thing with Keeper and made them both forget about her... This makes sense, I promise, I just got off the phone to Symon and needed to write it down before I forgot. I will make it more poetic, promise.
responses.allies:
Will flesh out, promise. 

Charlie.
Scrambles/Keeper.
Anichka.
Doc.
Cyrus.
responses.coterie: Charlie and Scrambles/Keeper. Will flesh out, promise.
responses.enemies:
Not enemies or rivals, but whilst human, Ivana unknowingly crossed paths with a number of Kindred, in ways that could be very problematic for them, when they realise who she is. 

Rene (Luca)
Cyrus 
Skinner - if he ends up showing up.

Will flesh out, promise.
responses.opposed_covenant:
Lance. Because ew. 

Again, will flesh out, promise.

mortal_family.description: Ivana has no living family she can turn to - they sold her on when she was quite young. She remembers echoes of her human life, but no one ever wanted her then, and no one would now. She assumes anyone who might have known her believes she is gone, and she has no desire to contact them... Certain circumstances notwithstanding...

boons_debts.description: Charlie.

responses.gaming_style_note:
My character will try focus on building power through the Turf War, fight and scheme in the gutters, and rise to praxis only after solidifying a base over time. 

Satisfaction = having things I do matter (not everything! just sometimes seeing how my efforts yield fruit)
responses.support_note: Assistance with managing complex rules will be greatly appreciated :)
responses.bloodline_rationale: We live in a world of surveillance. The perfect world for The Moulding Room to fester.
responses.court_motivation: Court is a wellspring of information, chances to accumulate intel on other kindred and kine alike, to be filed away, used and/or sold.
responses.ambitions_sydney: Sydney is a stepping stone, to gain power and prove a point -- that the Kine are as monstrous as Kindred, they just need to be shown the way.
responses.why_sydney: My (very secret) sponsor in The Moulding Room tracks up the chain to leadership in Palantir, which in fact is controlled by Kindred behind the scenes.  Sydney is a petri dish  (control group) to test mortal limits of horror and perversion. I've been given free reign to experiment with Kindred and Kine alike, to use the power of surveillance to gain power, and report back on my findings. Embrace the horror and ascend.
responses.why_covenant: I was recruited through the Embrace.
responses.covenant_goals: Secrets (of greater power and truths) and advancement within the Covenant.
responses.clan_goals: I am caught by my clan's traditions. I want to work faster -- achieve goals sooner. I lack the patience for the long long game.
responses.aspired_position: Socialite -- using my skills to build power and gain influence.
responses.view_traditions: Traditions matter until they don't.  I abide by the Traditions with the hope that someday we will no longer be bound by any of them.
responses.view_elysium: Yes. Always. Because someone is always watching...
responses.view_mortals: Playthings. Test subjects.
responses.embrace_story:
Jack is a failed adult film director -- failed film director generally. His trajectory was always downward:  dreams of becoming the next Spielgerg, reduced to commercials and ultimately adult films.  That was fine during the Golden Age of the adult film industry (1977-84), but things deteriorated quickly as low-quality video took over. (inspiration from Boogie Nights).

Jack's sire found him standing on the ledge of a building, ready to end it all. After listening to his story, Jack's sire deprived him of his agency one more time with the Embrace. It was violent, unwanted and resented.
responses.sire_story: My sire is active. I originally thought that my sire had turned me out as an act of sadism; but I later learned that he (and others in the Moulding Room) were very aware of me, had followed my Kine life for some time, and been nudging me along over a number of years, quietly interfering/obstructing my opportunities to lead me down the dark path of failure. I was a cultivated asset, which they now turn loose in Sydney.
responses.early_nights: Los Angeles. My early years were filled with violence and debauchery. Kine were playthings -- as were weaker Kindred.
responses.last_city_politics: Los Angeles had a very stable Praxis. My Sire and covenant thrived around the edges, providing support to the Prince (with dirt on just about everyone) in exchange for freedom from direct interference or oversight.
responses.touchstones: Classy/old/vintage cinemas ground my character. The only way he can experience human emotions now is through cinema.  He will go for a late night showing at the Dendy or Orpheum, sitting quietly off in the corner, and lose himself in the experience. A good film takes him ack to his youth when he dreamed of making the next great film about the human condition -- which is becoming further and further away, and harder and harder to connect with.
responses.hunting_style_note:
Never work with animals or children -- or eat them.  

Jack was a heavy user of cocaine back in the day and especially likes to feed off Kine who are high. He will hold and participate in raucous drug-fueled orgies and always offers narcotics to his intended victims.
responses.first_kill: His first kill was prepared for him by his Sire. Drunk and stoned, helpless -- and Jack was starving. He is haunted by her face, never forgetting it.  Now, he always makes sure that his victims are blindfolded, in case of over-feeding. He does not want any more ghosts haunting him.
responses.common_indulgences: Sex, drugs and cinema!
responses.allies: Not sure.  I think Jack is mainly a loner and will have to forge new alliances/relationships.
responses.coterie: No.
responses.enemies: None that I am aware of.
responses.opposed_covenant: The Lance. Jack didn't have much time for priests when he was Kine, he sees no need for them now, especially since he has learned about deeper (Crone) truths that go far beyond the Lance.
responses.intolerable_behaviours: Brutish behaviour -- Jack doesn't like bestial Gengrels very much.  They are just so crass.

mortal_family.description: Relatives probably alive -- but I have had no contact since I was Embraced.

responses.high_concept: Fallen Blood & Memory Addicted Elder: Ex-Invictus Lord turned Crone ecstatic.
responses.blood_potency: Have been repeatedly tororped to sleep off blood bonds and blood addiction while working for the Order of St Martin
responses.gaming_style_note: Social, Political and Mystical PvP alongside intense RP and horror.
responses.support_note: No support required.
responses.covenant_factions: None - primary drive is Mnemosyne bloodline
responses.court_motivation: Rebuild my reputation, become loved and respected, proliferate blood-sympathies, collect memories.
responses.ambitions_sydney: Spread the influence of my cult: The Remembrance of the Lidless Eye. Deepen my mastery of Cruac, and recover what remains of what memories were lost to the Dead Zone.
responses.why_sydney: Sent far away to stop rampant blood-sympathies polluting other continents. Dragged here with his protector/jailer Charlie Ballsack.
responses.why_covenant: I was tempted away by a Crone who was vastly more understanding of my blood addiction after being disgraced high-level Invictus. Within the Crone I came to worship Mnemosyne not just as an idea, but a god, now my lust for memories has only grown.
responses.covenant_goals: Become truly accepted, and thread the needle between the Crone and the Invictus (Order of St Martin). Regain my power, fear and respect as an Elder.
responses.clan_goals: He has none, my Bloodline though is my obsession, but I have no specific plans for it.
responses.aspired_position: Primogen - all respect, not responcibility.
responses.view_traditions: No fun, but I understand why. I've lived long enough to see what happens when we don't adhered to them.
responses.view_elysium: Always, I arbore physical violence.
responses.view_mortals: Objects for consumption and use.
responses.embrace_story: From English nobility, I was embraced under a falling meteorite, an omen of my expected potency and role. It was a formal and well attended affair. I was well groomed and prepared for my ascension.
responses.sire_story: Our memories are so mingled now that I am them and they are me. I am my Sire, I am also my Childe. We were an Elder of the Mnemosyne bloodline. We became the vessel of each other over time. They sleep now, but one day we shall become one again.
responses.early_nights: London in the late 1700's, it was a highly formalise and tightly held Invictus domain back then.
responses.last_city_politics: A stable Praxis held by a Triumvirate of Scions and the Order of St Martin in Eastern Europe. It slowed their hubris and made them weak.
responses.touchstones: My love, living eternally in me. Sometimes I pour her into a mortal that reminds me of her, and we dance again till she grows old. Maybe this kimono? One of us has been wearing it for a long time, I think.
responses.hunting_style_note: I generally feed on from the Mnemosyne cult, The Remembrance of the Lidless Eye, who give their blood willingly. Otherwise, I will walk the streets until someone calls to me, then I will cloud their mind and drink more from then than blood, for it is the memories I crave.
responses.first_kill: I am my first kill, I remember me giving myself to me, throat bared in the name of the Remembrance. I was old then, frail and close to death. I had lived a full and rich life and offered all that I was to me. For I wanted to live forever in the mind of a god, and I have.
responses.common_indulgences: Memories, sensations through blood-sympathy, vitae, opera, art, remembering, the cult, adoration.
responses.allies:
I have a grandchilde at court, Conrad Sondergaard of the Lancea
Anichka (Kaite) of the Crone.
responses.coterie: With Charlie Ballsack and Ivana Horvat. We share a Haven.
responses.enemies: Rene Meyer of the Carthians
responses.opposed_covenant: The Carthians, they are short sighted and infantile. I wouldn't actually care, but they grow vastly more potent with every decade, a vilely short period of time.
responses.intolerable_behaviours: Change, pushing for change, driving change. The world is chaos, and while it is beautiful, it's important to have something stable and unchanging to come back to.

mortal_family.description: My mortal family are 100's of years dead, but with the consumption of others' memories thousands of people are my family. I had breakfast with some of them yesterday morning before I went to my construction job. I love them all, but there are so many, one does not mourn the loss of a single grain of sand from the beach for long.

responses.blood_potency: 2 probably? I?ll be honest, I?m very new to this system, and would love some help with my
responses.gaming_style_note: I am a veteran RPG player, and pre Covid was active in the Combat LARP scene. I have loved the VTM world/World of Darkness setting for ages, and am excited for this experiance! I enjoy political intrigue, character-centric storytelling and getting absolutely dumpstered. Horror I?m middling on, but being the monster/boogieman sounds like a blast, though I think I?d enjoy more of a subtle manipulation enjoyer than PvP in a non-physical LARP, but may surprise myself. I?m reworking my character concept to be a major mover and shaker politically and I like my character as a concept related to power dynamics around Kindred existence.
responses.support_note: I?d prefer an attentive storyteller who integrates the characters role-play into the narrative, and have our actions or lack thereof effect the world. I am new to VTR, so would like some pointers OOC of a general nature occasionally.
responses.covenant_factions: Paladin of the Westminster Creed, serving an Inquisitor
responses.court_motivation:
Kirk?s duties in Sydney are twofold. Firstly, the protection of his Inquisitor, in general. He?s been inseparable from
Conrad for decades, viewing him as his spiritual leader, intellectual superior and benevolent benefactor. Secondly, there is intelligence pointing towards an assassin of the Crones known as the ?Grimalkin? that has been dispatched to thwart specifically the heretic hunters of the Sanctified. Kirk?s especially focused on them for the time-being. 

In court, these goals continue, with the added goal of establishing the Lance as a fixture within Sydney.
responses.ambitions_sydney:
Kirk?s duties in Sydney are twofold. Firstly, the protection of his Inquisitor, in general. He?s been inseparable from
Conrad for decades, viewing him as his spiritual leader, intellectual superior and benevolent benefactor. Secondly, there is intelligence pointing towards an assassin of the Crones known as the ?Grimalkin? that has been dispatched to thwart specifically the heretic hunters of the Sanctified. Kirk?s especially focused on them for the time-being.
responses.why_sydney: See above, but also: Indispensable to Inquisitor Sondergaard, whose aim is to safeguard the fledgling Damned within the Great Southern Land of the Holy Spirit.
responses.why_covenant: The Lance was there to forgive, anoint and grant him penance and absolution through violence. Also, as a superstitious person, the idea that a immortal who must feed on sentient beings to survive wouldn?t consider what would proceed past their Final Death confounds him.
responses.covenant_goals: Kirk wishes to ascend with his Inquisitor. As Brother Sondergaard becomes a Bishop, then Cardinal, Kirk wants to rise to the rank of Crusader, at his side.
responses.clan_goals: He wants to get to know more Gangrel, and wants to reconnect with a clan he?s never had. However, as a ?God-botherer?, most Gangrel see him as deeply strange. This said, if his intelligence is correct, the Grimalkin (the assassin he is tracking) is a Gangrel? something he can maybe use against them??
responses.aspired_position: An Enforcer, as he protects Inquisitor Sondergaard, who he sees as his Primogen.
responses.view_traditions: The Traditions were introduced to Kirk late in his unlife. He has only followed them for a relatively short time, but understands them as church doctrine, and is grateful that his previous sins have been forgiven.
responses.view_elysium: He respects it openly, as it makes life easier. He will resort to intimidation before Disciplines, but the burning faith of his broken Beast doesn?t concern itself with petty social norms, and the Human can do only so much against the internal righteous incandescent indignation at the indefensible indecency of the Second Estates? detractors.
responses.view_mortals: Mortals and half-mortals to Kirk are pitiful, broken things. He sees the weakness which plagued him in them, and despises them and commiserates for them in equal measure.
responses.embrace_story:
As the social unrest that would lead to the Troubles occurred in Belfast, Kirk joined a fledgling organisation within the Ulster Defence Association (UDA) he simply knew as ?The Cavalry?, and wanted to protect Irish Protestants and re-unify Ireland under English rule. Slowly radicalised, Kirk was Embraced by his father, who became a leader figure within the group, as a Gangrel Paladin of the Crimson Cavalry. To be initiated, he burned down a  Catholic monastery as a show of faith, and committed many such crimes against kine and kindred, and worse.

The Embrace itself was largely utilitarian. Just another rite of passage, to his knowledge. A show of loyalty. Nothing inside him and no-one told him what he was submitting too, and he didn?t even see his father behind him, fangs bared, as he was staring at the crucifix in the underground bunker that served as the chapel of the Crimson Cavalry in Ulster, rededicating his life to the protection of the Church of Christ and it?s saints.
responses.sire_story:
Destroyed. 

Seeing the Irish Civil War, and the atrocities committed by the IRA, Kirk?s father moved to Ireland from England and joined the Royal Irish Constabulary (RIC), otherwise known as the ?Black and Tans?. As a young man, Kirk idolised his father and the struggle for control over Ireland against the rebels, and vowed one day to ?protect? Ireland himself.

However, once Kirk defected and condemned the Crimson Cavalry to the Second Estates ministrations, he returned to Belfast with Inquisitor Sondergaard and dedicated himself to hunting the heretics who inducted him to the night. With Conrad?s expertise and experience, Kirk?s bitter contempt for his old coterie was inexorable. Weakened as the Cavalry were by repeatedly violating the Tradition of Progeny to expand their ranks, the Inquisitor and his retinue cut a bloody swathe through their ranks, until Kirk faced down his sire. Having been made a Sorcerous Eunuch by the Lancea et Sanctus, Kirk caught his sire unaware, and pulverised him to unrecognisablity. With his sire?s last strength, he commanded Kirk to diablerise him, cementing his damnation, his final conquest over his wayward Childe.

Conrad, however, arrived at the last opportune moment, and literally kicked Kirk off his sire like a misbehaving mongrel. Strangling his Frenzy, Kirk cowed to his compatriot, and allows his sire to meet the Final Death at Conrad?s hands.
responses.early_nights:
Kirk?s early nights were full of indoctrination and bitter training. He was forged into a tool of his father, and made into a weapon for the Cavalry?s purposes, committing many attractions against the splinter IRA hold-outs, surviving only do his undead resiliency multiple times. In Ulster, the Catholics whispered the name ?The Hound of Heaven? with bitter fear.

However, as the Troubles began in earnest, Kirk became increasingly disquieted by the racially and sex-preference discriminatory aspects of the Crimson Cavalry?s doctrine. His resolve broke when he was cajoled by his father to murder a pregnant Jewish kine, a refugee from the Holocaust, purely for ?racial purity? of the kine herd rather than feeding. Distraught by his actions, and with little knowledge of the broader Requiem, Kirk fled the organisation and hid from his father and the rest of the Cavalry by travelling to London, trying to keep a low profile and avoid ongoing Trouble?s chaos.

Despite how well he hid, a pack of lupine known as the Ivory Claws or ?The Pure? were present in the area and were in serious danger of causing a Masquerade breach with how bold their movements were, causing an Inquisitor of the Lancea et Sanctum by the name of Conrad Sondergaard to come across him as a suspect, given his canine aspect. Taking the Childe under his wing, the young Inquisitor was surprised by his combat experience and tenacity, and allowed Kirk to track the werewolves and eliminate them along with the parishes Paladins and Crusaders. When pushed on his parish and sire, Conrad enlisted Kirk to join him in holding the Crimson Cavalry to account.
responses.last_city_politics: London: Tyrannical Invictus city, with a Lancea minority.
responses.touchstones:
- A bastard sword he took as a trophy from an Ivory Claw lupine he tracked and slew. The Beast quietens at it?s victory, slinking back as  his Human touches the cold metal.
- A Star of David pendant of the kine whose death set his life on a fresh path. He prays for her soul often, and wishes he knew her name. The shame of his actions burns in the Human over the Beasts rage.
responses.hunting_style_note: For feeding, Kirk tends to foster connections with young, disadvantaged young men as something of a Christian ?Scene Queen?. He sees his former self in his prey, and though he refuses to admit it to himself in his celibacy, his sexual preferences tend towards them as well. A large, gruff, silent man with quiet zealotry, such youths gravitate to him for his understated ?realness? in amongst the sickly-sweet rhetoric of the kine?s theology. Small Bible-studies, faith retreats and men?s sheds form the basis of his feeding rituals, disguising his feeding as rapturous benediction of the Holy Spirit within private, one-on-one prayer circles.
responses.first_kill:
His first kill was long before he was Embraced. In the Irish Civil War, there were no clear civilians or soldiers, just an underbelly of rancid hatred across all levels of society.

The first time his Beast rose up and slew who he was feeding on was a foot soldier in the former IRA. Feeding on a young, pretty man, the human slipped as it found the intimacy of the extraction of vitae become sexually charged, and the Beast drank greedily and deep with the added ecstasy of the moment. Kirk could never admit to himself of his homosexual urges, and has hid it from everyone in his life.
responses.common_indulgences: Despite being part of the Westminster Creed, Kirk likes the traditions of the High Anglican Communion, or the Catholic Mass, or even the Monarchal Creed. While he doesn?t believe it?s necessary to serve Longinus or the Holy Father, it?s plodding, tested surety calms him. Part of him seeks penance from the ways of his youth, and seeks connections with the Catholics who he so hated.
responses.allies: Since the night Conrad slew his father, Kirk Grimm has been Inquisitor Sondergaard?s baleful shadow. A hound of heaven, a church grim, a perros de guerra for the Lancea et Sanctum covenant, being the personal paladin for his ?Boss?. They have tangled often with the Circle of the Crone, the Second Estate?s antithetical covenant. While they?ve come up against other covenants, Kirk has no strong feelings against them, however, he despises the ?witchlings? as he calls them.
responses.coterie: Yes, but it?s a small coterie, to my knowledge. Just Kirk and Conrad.
responses.enemies: Yes, ?Grimalkin? is my mate Jono. He?s tentative about coming, due to the circumstances of his life and how busy he is, but we can explore that plot if he ever arrives.

responses.character_name: Macheath, ?Mac?. ?That fangless fuck that broke my fucking legs?
responses.high_concept: Irish Republican child soldier turned urban gorilla.
responses.gaming_style_note: This time I really want to lean into causing chaos and seeing what happens. I don?t want to ruin other people?s fun but my last character was a hardcore institutionalist and while that was fun and on-brand, I think Vampire works better when the old Traditions and institutions aren?t as stable as they pretend. Action is cool in theory but I?m happy for lots of streamlining there to avoid the slog of combat with a billion people acting. I do want to make violence feel more immediate and serious to ground things and avoid a superhero-y or just ?coalition of the weird? vibe; vampires existence only through violence and I want to make that unavoidable. Other than that I obviously want to play with and alongside Jess because it?s a shared activity for us.
responses.support_note: Mostly I just want to be able to work with STs to keep the game fun for everyone. I?m happy to be poked if I can support the narrative or make someone?s game more interesting, and to be able to suggest things to accomplish that to the STs. I don?t really care about ?winning?, but keeping things moving and exciting is important.
responses.covenant_factions: Mac doesn?t perfectly line up with any specific faction, though he?s somewhat similar to the Saboteurs and has weird anti-overlap with the AOA. Where the AOA rejects vampire involvement with mortals, Mac instead sees involvement in mortal affairs as a sort of duty. He doesn?t see vampirism as mystical or special, it?s just another set of talents he should be leveraging for the ideals he believes in.
responses.bloodline_rationale: The Norvegi are the all-singing, all-dancing scum of the earth; whipping boys for the great and the good. They?re the working stiffs of the kindred, and Mac has been at the bottom of the social pile since birth. The brutality and secret talents of the bloodline really match his vibe and approach.
responses.court_motivation: Working with and protecting Maggie; preventing any kindred or faction from gaining ascendancy; presenting threat; working out what the enemy are up to.
responses.ambitions_sydney:
Smash the ladder and reject traditional vampiric hierarchies. Anarchy. Liberation. Freedom?whether they like it or not. 

This is not utopian idealism, or at least not pacifistic. Mac is very comfortable with and accustomed to violence. His principles are real but they do more to justify violence than prevent it.
responses.why_sydney:
After years of travelling, fighting and smuggling, the lure of land without existing tyrannies, hierarchies and petty despots ignited a kind of hope within Mac that had died with the end of real (aka armed) struggle for a united Ireland. He knows that Australia has been a haven for many Irish migrants, and has its own history of violence and colonisation. Perhaps the dream isn?t dead. Perhaps something better is possible. 

Perhaps Botany Bay is the new foggy dew.
responses.why_covenant: Growing up in Belfast between the declaration of the Irish State and the Troubles, Mac was always going to be a republican, and has been fighting the British in one way or other since British soldiers crippled his father when he was 12. His sire offered him the power to help his people, and while she wasn?t as honest as she could have been about the costs, the Carthian Movement offered the same sort of promise the IRA had. Mac is a true believer, and he?s committed to a vision of vampiric society that wouldn?t even be considered in another covenant.
responses.covenant_goals:
Flatten everything. Smash every throne. Shatter every crown. Break every chain. Protect Maggie. Hurt anyone that gets in the way. Remove those who do it too often. 

The Carthians should be powerful, and should sue that power to prevent hierarchies or rulers; even their own members.
responses.clan_goals:
On the face of it Mac isn?t anything like the Mekhet stereotype. He?s obviously, loud and unsubtle. He?ll do dirty jobs and solve problems fist-first. 

But he?s not as simple as he looks, and he knows the value of committing to a costume. When he?s not at court, the affectations drop. The long hair gets buzzed off every night, and the beard often goes with it. He watches, records, plans. He prepares. Then he acts, suddenly and without warning. If he finds likeminded Mekhet, he?ll find a way to make them useful.
responses.aspired_position: Mac is more of an enforcer than anything else, but his main goal is tearing down those who set themselves above the rest.
responses.view_traditions: Mac views the Traditions as practical rules that are generally sensible, but that?s the limit of his respect. He doesn?t care much about laws in general, and he doesn?t see Vampire laws as special.
responses.view_elysium: Pragmatically and publicly, yes. But he?s not averse to exploiting those rules or breaking them to achieve his goals. He?ll also walk right up to the limits of what?s permitted at Elysium, or retaliate for what happens at Elysium the next night if he feels it?s warranted or necessary.
responses.view_mortals:
Mac still thinks he?s close to humanity. He rarely feeds on mortals, other than those he sees as enemies or deserving punishment, because his bloodline make feeding on animals and cold blood - even spilled, spoiled blood - safer and easier. He still sees humans as natural peers and sees their conflicts as equally as important (if not more) than those of kindred. 

He don?t have long before he?ll have to confront the reality that he?s a long way from humanity, but for now he stays around humans and gets involved in their affairs more than most vampires ever would even consider.
responses.embrace_story: Mac?s embrace was, like most of his life and unlife, practical. His sire drained him to a desiccated husk, fed him her blood, buried him under 3 feet of earth in a cellar 30 feet below the ground and then came back the next night to watch him dig his way back out.
responses.sire_story:
Karoline Lenya was herself a young vampire when she Embraced Mac, and she gave him the name ?Macheath? which he has used since to avoid his mortal family becoming targets. German and working-class, she never spoke about her own sire, but described the Norvegi bloodline as a tool of revolutionary power, exploiting their degraded status within Kindred society to uncover weaknesses and secrets that their ?betters? failed to conceal from such pathetic dregs. 

Throughout Mac?s early years, she forged bonds between Carthians and mortal revolutionaries, strengthening weapon pipelines from the Middle East to Germany and Northern Ireland. She went into torpor in the 90s to escape kindred enemies closing in after she diablerised an Invictus elder, just before the Good Friday Accords.
responses.early_nights:
Belfast, at the start of the Troubles. A city at war by day and night; Carthians supporting Republican militants while Unionist militias and the Royal Ulster Constabulary did the bidding of geriatric Invictus landlords. Survival that?s as much a matter of avoiding the enemy as feeding, and the Masquerade stretches to breaking point as hungry, angry kindred join the mortal violence. 

For Mac, kindred existence is as defined by violent conflict as his mortal life was. He views political violence as normal, expected, even routine. He?s not trigger-happy or careless, but he doesn?t hesitate to leverage tremendous violence if he thinks it?s warranted.
responses.last_city_politics: Having spent most of the last 2 decades travelling, Mac has seen the spectrum of cities. What defines his recent experience is starting fires. Local Carthians learned that when Doc and Mac were in town, it was time to shake the tree. Mac isn?t much of Carthian Lawyer, but he?d learned a few tricks that could turn a gilded crown into a royal noose. Whatever local resistance there was suddenly found themselves able to shift the balance of power, and if there wasn?t a resistance yet, Maggie made sure there was one by the time they left.
responses.touchstones: Mac?s niece Fiona is the matriarch of the Australian branch of the family. She?s never met him, but he found her when he got to Sydney, and keeps an eye on her and her clan from a distance. She?s the spitting image of his mother at the same age, and he can?t shake the feeling that his family are going to need him again [NB this is very clearly projection]. No-one but Maggie knows - as far as he can tell - and he avoids doing anything that might endanger them. Except, of course that he still keeps tabs, and that puts them in more danger than they would otherwise ever face.
responses.hunting_style_note: As much as he can, Mac feeds on animals and discarded blood. He?ll clean abattoir killing floors, boxing rings or surgical suites when he can, and catch strays when he can?t. When he has to, he?ll lean on his pull in the movement to get access to another Carthian?s herd, or feed on mortals he might be targeting for other reasons (supporting mundane Allies and Contacts, for example). His Norvegi heritage makes non-violent feeding almost impossible, and he?s steadfastly avoiding the question of what will happen as his blood continues to thicken.
responses.first_kill:
Mac?s first kill was in his mortal life. He almost never kills when feeding, or even feeds on mortals, but when mortals are the targets of violence already it?s easy to justify feeding on them. 

He doesn?t clearly remember the first time it happened. Body wracked and shattered from a fight with another Kindred, he was overcome by the Beast and came back to himself holding a drained, desiccated corpse as the bony spines retracted into his hands. He?s pretty sure it was an Orangeman. It would have to have been. They were everywhere that night. Definitely an enemy. 

(The one small silver lining of the idiosyncratic feeding mechanism of a Norvegi is that a drained victim passes as mummified, which helps avoid obvious Masquerade breaches of the victim can be hidden somewhere that delays discovery.)
responses.common_indulgences: Mac defines himself around work and conflict, but music has become an essential part of Mac?s Requiem. Rebel songs, folk music snarling punk tracks, heavy metal?anything that feels alive and says some kind of fuck you to the blue bloods and fat cats. And while he?d never admit to it, he?s got a soft spot to musicals - mostly out of nostalgia from his time with his sire.
responses.allies:
Maggie is the closest companion Mac?s ever had, and the only coterie-mate he?s had. 

He?s worked with or encountered Einar, Rene Meyer and Eve enough in the past to have some sense of their character, and he defaults to a basic level of trust with other Carthians until he has a reason not to. 

Charlie Ballsack is probably the only Invictus he doesn?t immediately want to throw into a furnace, largely because Charlie doesn?t seem very committed to the First Estate, and because he feels like Nosferatu are fellow travellers as far as their blood drags them down the hierarchy. They?re not friends, but they?ve crossed paths before without killing each other. 

Mac and Maggie met Ivana before she was Embraced; she doesn?t remember them but Mac feels a little obligated to keep an eye on her.
responses.coterie:
Maggie and Mac have been a coterie of two for a long time, initially brought together by ideology but now by long-term trust and companionship. 

Mac also tend to encourage other Carthians to all behave like a coterie, though, because he knows the importance of collective action and collective responsibility.
responses.enemies:
Kirk Grimm, Paladin of the Lance

The conflict:

Well, Kirk?s breaking point was when he was put on a detail to cull the ?mud people? the Cavalry wanted gone. He found a women with child who wore a Star of David, and decided that she wasn?t too far gone, and let her go. His sire found out, and using Vinuculum, forced him to hunt her down and kill her. Not for vitae, just for the racial purity of the kine.

Mac and Kirk?s actual encounter was more coincidental than planned. Chaotic streets, sporadic gunfire, smell of burning rubber in the air?

There?s sort of running street fights happening and they end up facing off in some narrow back alley. Both already on-edge and hungry from extended fighting and they end up either frenzying or right on the edge of it. Total masquerade breach shit if a mortal had stumbled onto them; scrambling on busted legs, Mac with an arm all but hanging off, Kirk swinging an axe with a visibly shattered elbow, both with exposed bone and lethal head wounds. At some point they just can?t continue; the sun is rising and they both end up desperately going to ground to avoid the dawn. 

Mac fully intends to kill Kirk when he gets the chance.
responses.opposed_covenant: The Invictus! Bow-tie fascists and imperial motherfuckers. Nothing worse than wannabe aristos throwing their weight around. They?re the antithesis of everything Mac believes in (and he believes in it a lot). In Mac?s (some simple) worldview, the Invictus is the avatar of evil. Individual members might be less awful, but he really sees the covenant and the vision of kindred society it represents as unsalvageable and deserving of utter destruction.
responses.intolerable_behaviours:
Mac has somewhat idiosyncratic tolerances. He doesn?t like violence targeted at his allies, but he doesn?t really have a principled problem with violence up to and including killing, so long as the target is a combatant of some sort (being a member of a covenant is sufficient to qualify) or otherwise has committed some offence deserving of punishment. He?s especially intolerant of violence towards innocent humans (but again, innocence isn?t all that precise a category). 

He can?t stand slavery or indentured servitude, but doesn?t have qualms about blackmail, intimidation and other hard forms of leverage. 

It?s basically an elaborate version of ?All?s fair in love and war?, and it reflects that Mac has a sort of double intensity of the normal ?frozen in time? experience most Kindred share: first from his own forced maturity and truncated childhood, then again from his Embrace in the midst of a bitter civil conflict.

mortal_family.description: Mac has avoided contact with his mortal family for decades to keep them safe, but after his mother?s death he discovered that a branch of the family had migrated to Australia. For the first time in years, he?s keeping tabs on them. He knows he probably shouldn?t, but the desperate fear that he?s turning into the old bastards he hates is gnawing at his soul, and he needs something to anchor himself. See the touchstone entry below.

boons_debts.description: Rene Meyer owes Mac a major boon for digging her out of bombsite. Mac behaves as though he doesn?t even think of it; that?s a facade. He keeps close track.
