responses.gaming_style_note:
Something I've found myself enjoying is a hefty amount of intrigue in context of the broader world (Such as two kindred fighting for territory though proxies of two different clubs) indesperced with moments of direct confrontation that has a chance to escalate. As much as I enjoy a good mystery and subtly working my way around the rules and laws of Elysium, I prefer having opportunities to cut loose and throw everything I have at someone- and in turn be hit with everything they have.

Basically, I think what I'd like to see is an aggressive kind of game where action (and dealing with the consequences of said action) is encouraged, rather than a more passive and reserved style of play. Be it physical altercation or social- I like things happening. 

Now that's probably going to be up to me and how I play, but that's going to be my mindset going in. If I don't take at least a few risks this game that could get my character in mortal (Or immortal) peril, then I've not achieved my goal.
responses.support_note:
Touchstones are a new very interesting mechanic that I'd like some attention paid to- I know it's not a PVE game similar to what we had previous chronicle, but given touchstones are tied to a character interacting with them is more PVP than not, I feel.

That being said, as confident as I play my character sometimes I might require a little bit of reassurance in the midst of high-stakes political machinations.
responses.bloodline_rationale:
The doctrine of the AOA goes against nature of Kindred- it's a cause that is likely doomed from the very start, moreso than a lot of other Carthian philosophies. If there's a bloodline that would still pursue a lost cause, it's Zelani- as they've got a knack for one in a million odds. 

Rene has failed upwards many times- misfortunes that prove to be beneficial in the end, at least, with how I'm putting together the backstory. Zelani would work great with that theme.
responses.court_motivation:
Ren relies upon information gleaned at court to know what's going on around town. She hasn't the extensive network that other kindred have- no ghoul informants or institution connections- she also relies on court to fraternize, given her inability to trespass uninvited getting on peoples good side is an important aspect of her work.

Also, it's a good way of shrugging off the heat she accumulates in her subversive AOA actions- being a present and friendly face will allow her hopefully shirk some suspicion. She needs to know the throats to cut, after all.
responses.ambitions_sydney:
Ideally, the cessation of all kindred meddling in the city. Sydney is a gift, a place where mortals have been able to freely explore ideas without the restrictive and narrow-minded influence of kindred.

Knowing that's probably not going to be achieved, at least some advancement in the Anti-Obstructionist cause. Even if it takes her life to do it.
responses.why_sydney: Sent by those she knew in the AOA of London, she's unsure if it was gifted to her as an opportunity to advance the cause in a vital piece of virgin territory... or to just get rid of a troublesome kindred.
responses.why_covenant: Sired into the Covenant, she stayed the course due to her distaste for all the others. She doesn't get along with other Carthians sometimes, but a majority of them are at least self-aware enough not to be completely tiresome.
responses.covenant_goals: Drag it further towards the radicalism of the AOA- kicking and screaming if she has to.
responses.clan_goals:
Herself and her fellow cousins have the biggest issue of all the clans staying out of mortal business- she's aware of that, and will have to wrestle with that fact during her stay in Australia, lest she get too connected and betray her cause. 

Still, she at least hopes to slap a few hands and make sure they all behave enough not to risk the very fragile masqurade in this city.
responses.aspired_position: Ideally, an Enforcer such as the Sherif or Hound would give her a perfect opertunity and excuse to perpetuate her goals.
responses.view_traditions: Ren is a big fan of the first- the Masqurade goes a ways in protecting the development of human society from the numerous would-be vampire god-kings. The others, she's not as fussed about. Though she's never broken the third- there is a dark part of her that's curious about the possibility, and given her line of work she might have opportunity for it... however, as jaded as she is, kindred have their own rights, that must be respected. Such a monstrous fate does not befit anyone.
responses.view_elysium: She would like to be seen as a very respectful member of court- though will break any and all laws to advance her ideology.
responses.view_mortals:
Mortals are for more than just Vitae, as they provide something vampires cannot- innovation. Many kindred don't realize this as you cannot fill your belly with ideas, but their without the constant influx of mortal musings they'd still be stuck in the dark ages, and they wouldn't have any of the new leisures that even kindred now enjoy. There is more to life than hunting and hunted, otherwise you might as well just give in to the beast. 

Ghouls are an example of kindred giving in to that temptation, broadening personal power whilst sacrificing the broader potential of all. They must be disincentivized, by force if necessary. If possible, ghouls should be detoxed and released into the wider world (After their minds have been scrubbed enough, or have been given a place where their stories will be ignored), however more than likely they will need to be killed to stop the influence of their domitor from spreading further.

The AOA does not make compromises.
responses.embrace_story:
Ren was promised much, and got almost none of it. Her Sire, a Daeva called Oscar, used 'The Wish' to show her the potent power available to her if she would allow her embrace. He could have taken her if he wished, but wanted her to come to him.

After advancing her efforts in the industrial activism of London, she was ready to accept his gift and take it even further. Meeting in his luxury estate, his lavish nature didn't set off any alarm bells until it was way too late. After her embrace, all her efforts was towards Oscar's advancement- and with her dying dream she grew bitter enough to take up the AOA's cause.
responses.sire_story:
Oscar, a Daeva Carthian currently in London who's mostly occupied with finding new ways to enjoy himself. He expects news from Australia, in the hopes that when the aura of death recedes he might explore this new land himself. 

He embraced Ren because she was useful, and someone he could load the more bothersome aspects of undeath upon- she acted, basically, as his secretary. As a Zelani he mostly flew though life with luck and charm, and though she inherited these traits she was working for two.

Though she has distanced herself from Oscar over the years, he still expects her occasional abeyance- and gets it less because she's loyal, but because he has a very frustrating habit of finding the best time to ask for it, using the best sort of leverage. She doesn't like him, he doesn't really think about her.
responses.early_nights:
Ren was embraced in London in the midst of a great conflict between Carthians and Lancea. She was originally embraced as a tool to further the influence of the movement, and thus has always had an adversarial relationship with the Lancea Sanctum. 

Given the environment she was fostered in, she sees vampire society as mainly violent and treacherous- which just might be true. However, the more calm and respectful machinations of other courts is a foreign concept to her- and she has a habit of taking things up to 11.
responses.last_city_politics:
London was ruled by Invictus until the turn of the century, where their influence started to rapidly decline. Tied for second, the Carthian Movement and Lancea Sanctum rushed to fill the space- this conflict is still going, and is now spilling out to Australia.

Weather this is just a plan of the Invictus or they are indeed dwindling in power, is unsure. Perhaps the Crone is behind it. All that matters is that there's been many attempts at a stable rule over London, and thus far it's not worked.
responses.touchstones:
Despite Ren's attitude towards humanity, she feels that it's still important to stay connected  with them- as long as that connection is not in a dominative capacity. Having lost touch with many of the touchstones she held in London, she set to work finding anchor-points to keep her stable...

The first, which she has since started to neglect since her arrival to Australia, is a pen-pal she has selfishly kept from her life. They know her by a pen name, and though their communication is sporadic at times a letter has always come. They are old now, Ren is sure- and she doesn't know what she'll do when they die- which is in part why she's made distance with them. Still, they speak of times when she still breathed, and that was enough to keep her on the straight and narrow. She has yet to reply to the most recent letter.

The second, is a horrible capitalist of a man who owns a local apartment complex. She hates him truly- but not with the immortal side of her that rouses the beast and hungers for blood, no, her hate for this man is purely human. With each exploitative measure he takes against his tenants, each penny pinched, she's reminded that not all monotonousness is monopolized by kindred.

The third, is a dockworker whom is currently laboring under an exploitive contract for an oil company. Peter Rhone, whom she made sure to join at his bar of choice every few nights, complained about his current predicament and long work hours. She could easily help the man, with both her mundane and supernatural skills- but to do so would ruin what connected her to him. His struggle, used to be hers- and seeing what he fought against and what he gave in to roused memories of her own frustration. She loves him like a brother, and hopes that one day he'll find a way to help himself that does not involve the path she took.
responses.hunting_style_note:
Ren is not above seduction, as much as she'd like to present a prideful face the hunger of Daeva is fierce. Finding it easiest to cultivate faux-trysts, she tries to be done with the grizzly business as soon as she can- often poaching other territories to do so.

This style results from the desperate fear of getting attached. Keeping true to her ideals is easy on a full stomach, but she tries her best to know as little of her meals as possible before fleeing in their sleep of the kiss.
responses.first_kill:
Her first kill was in service to her Sire- having been sent on a variety of errands, he eventually asked her to intimidate an inconvenient civilian. Doing so, she was unprepared to face the sheer obstinacy of the woman- and as she levied insult and ire against Ren, the kindred was drove to fury- killing her in frenzy. It was something she'd not been well taught to contain, the Zelani bloodline already quite prone to anger. 

She had at least cleaned scenes like this before for her sire, but it still shook her. When she told Oscar what had happened, he was unconcerned- perhaps a little pleased. The job had been done, and likely he wasn't even aware it was her very first victim.
responses.common_indulgences:
Ren loves art galleries, movies, and most anything avant-garde. She tries to paint when she can, though is always frustrated with thoughts of her nature- if Kindred really cannot create as humans can, is her work just a reproduction? Does it count? If it does, and if what she's making really is of her own work, then can kindred actually create of their own accord? And if so, how much?

She tries not to think about very much, and has a nice spot on a dock crane that she's dragged an easel onto.
responses.allies:
Einar Solveig - Her closest ally, though they don't know each-other very well they share unity in purpose, likewise she has been sent to keep an eye on him. The Carthians of London think him too reckless, but the AOA might have use of him if he could keep on track enough.

Regardless of politics, she sees somewhat of a kindred spirit in the man- and would likely share his attitude of autonomy if she hadn't grown into this firebrand. She's also wary, considering his abilities and history, that she would do well to make sure Einar *stays* an ally.

Eve- A fellow Daeva, Ren inducted Eve into the Carthian Movement, and though she is wary of the connections Eve still is held by she's hoping they turn out a good egg.

Doc & Macheath - Fellow Carthians and revolutionaries, they're aware of her AOA sympathies. Macheath has pulled her out of the proverbial fire in the past, and Doc has patched her up, so she's disposed to trusting the two further than what she normally might others... though there's a certain amount of patroniziation implicit in the dynamic, given that Doc and Macheath are much more successful firebrands by all accounts.

Charlie Ballsack - Associates in dour business, she respects him substantially- and being quite honest, fears him a little more. Despite how violently he conducts his business, it's certainly honest. Her positive feelings towards the man are also loaded with the fact that he's aware of her more heinous acts, and has witnessed her wavering in her commitment to the Carthian Cause
responses.coterie: Her current Coterie is with Einar Solveig
responses.enemies:
Conrad Sondergaard - a policeman who she butted heads with in London, whom they have a mutual dislike that grew into a sort of hatred over the decades. Out of all the Lancea of London (Who also often share a dislike for Rene) Conrad is the most personal.

Keeper - A meddler of mortal practices, they've had a bit of an issue with eachother ever since she trashed a cult hall of his. He also destroyed an artistic movement of some fondness for Rene.
responses.opposed_covenant: The Lancea Sanctum, though inadvertent circumstance, has become Ren's main rival. Though the Invictus and Lance both share meddlesome practices towards kine, in London she had mainly been fighting against the Lancea and a habit is hard to break.
responses.intolerable_behaviours: As stated a few times, the Ren is very close to the philosophy of the AOA- Ghouling is a practice she will not forgive, and though she overlooks it when members of her covenant engage in it, it's only for the practicality that you can't be fighting all fronts at once. She will, at least, let her displeasure be known.

mortal_family.description: Musings of visiting her family back in France plagued Ren, until the moment she joined the AOA. After which, she disdained such hypocritical thoughts and ensured the mortal Ren was known to be dead, severing her ties to her mortal life in an act of ritual.

boons_debts.description:
Cyrus Renold, Major Boon- Ren has inherited this boon from her Sire, who owed it to Cyrus' Sire. Though she is not aware that it is Cyrus that holds it, she is to do a favor for anyone who presents her with this promise and proof thereof. 

Doc & Macheath, Major Boon each- During her time in Ireland she was rescued and brought back to health by these two after an unfortunate conflict with a ghoul. 

Charlie Ballsack, Major Boon- Charlie stepped in to do a job that Rene was supposed to do after she pleaded for his help. She owes him big time.

Eve, Minor (Owed to Rene)- this one is unconfirmed, but given that Rene was the one that brought Eve into the movement it's likely there's something there.

responses.gaming_style_note: Personal horror, the internalisation of a characters inherent contradictions
responses.support_note: I am familiar with VTM5 but not Requiem2. I am open to whatever spare help can be offered
responses.court_motivation: The approval of my foster-sire. I will show him that he picked something of worth out of the gutter, not a random piece of detritus. I will help him secure control of Sydney
responses.ambitions_sydney: I want to see how far this Beast can take me. I want to feel my cheeks brush against the borders of what is attainable in undeath
responses.why_sydney: Under the command of my foster-sire. I'm here to connect with any other Invictus in the area and begin laying the groundwork for my lieges seizure of power
responses.why_covenant: I spent my life being a gig-job shit kicker, I'm not going to spend another (much longer) lifetime doing that again. My foster-sire is the template, and I will grow to fit it
responses.covenant_goals: Advancement and (secretly) approval from the Invictus royalty
responses.clan_goals: I couldn't care less about the Gangrel bloodline as some ideal or cultural group. I can recognise, though, how our uncanny closeness with the Beast is what gives me my monstrous edge.
responses.aspired_position: I seek the seat of a ruler, though realistically I'm suited to be an Enforcer
responses.view_traditions: How I see them doesn't matter. My foster-sire has a stance on them; his stance is my stance. In his absence, I suppose idealistically I don't see much of a use for them, except Amaranth, that shits fucked up thats like, cannibalism but somehow worse. Progeny who gives a shit; nobody needed a license to have kids when I was alive, and Masquerade I guess is important but like, is it REALLY important? Just the thought of us terrifies people, would kine really do shit if they knew we were real? (OoC: Ryan is young - a lot of his views on vampire society are painted with the same palette he used in life. But Ryan is ultimately not a hugely idealistic man, he wants power and wants to work for those in power, so if that means he needs to make a show of upholding the Traditions because that's what a good Invictus does, he will)
responses.view_elysium: I honor it, not because it deserves to be honored, but because it is the wish of my foster-sire, and I don't want to embarrass myself and by extension, the Invictus
responses.view_mortals: Rider's of coat-tails. They would be pathetic if they weren't instead so pitiable
responses.embrace_story: It was violent. A Doordash delivery gone wrong. I ran into my sire in a back alley and they accidentally drained me dry. Attempting to right their wrong, they turned me rather than leaving me for dead
responses.sire_story: Morgan Pennfold. I hate their guts. I haven't seen them in about a year
responses.early_nights: New Zealand. Quiet as far as kine activity goes kindred activity goes. I was able to stretch my legs and explore undeath myself. At least until I upset the one vampire in my city worth a damn; my now foster-sire Aleksander Ambrose
responses.last_city_politics: A small demesne in Christchurch, ruled by Aleksander Ambrose
responses.touchstones: My old phone. It was broken before I joined the Invictus, when my foster-sire punished me severely for disrupting their business. It serves as a reminder of who I was, and why that childe deserved to be broken
responses.hunting_style_note: I pretend to enjoy it when a superior can provide me with a docile victim. But secretly, I love the thrill of predating on kine. I use my knowledge of streets and back alleys to find isolated targets and close in on them. I like the sourness of cortisol-tainted blood so I make sure my victims know I'm there before I pounce, overpowering them and taking my fill. As much as I love to leave a can empty, it's a habit I'm trying to get out of now that I've got others who depend on me to not make the Invictus look bad
responses.first_kill: It was one of my first nights after leaving behind my blood-sire. He warned me about the dangers of giving in to the beast, but he was a pathetic nobody, and I could feel the beast revving inside me, firing on all cylinders. So I pressed down the pedal, enjoyed the ride, and saw how far this thing could take me. This was the night I caught the attention of my foster-sire, and what earned me my first round of 'discipline' from him
responses.common_indulgences: As much as I'm loathe to admit it, there's parts of me that miss the late nights out on the road, even if it was spent as a borderline slave delivering peoples food for them. I have fond memories of long nights sat in my car on foggy roads just listening to my favourite songs, and I'm not ready to give those memories up just yet
responses.allies: My duty here is to lay the groundwork for Invictus rulership. Though at odds with what I personally want, I will make whatever alliances best advance my covenants cause (I am a new player and don't know other players characters. However I am not a player that uses their character to explain why the WONT do things. I am a player that looks for reasons as to why, despite their best interests, my character engages with the game
responses.coterie: Not currently, no
responses.enemies: I would like to get a feel for other players and characters before I feel comfortable answering this.
responses.opposed_covenant: The Carthian's. I see in them who I used to be, and I hate it
responses.intolerable_behaviours: Betrayal of an agreement you've made. Insubordination within consenting hierarchy

mortal_family.description: My family is still alive, they are probably wondering where I am. They wouldn't believe me if I told them

boons_debts.description: I'm looking at selling off the place I'm staying at to help provide a lodge for us Invictus. Regardless of whether I'm officially in the First Estate or not, thats got to count for something

responses.high_concept: Charming Bogan with Long Distance Stockholm Syndrome Rediscovers Autonomy??
responses.gaming_style_note: Oh some horror would be hectic. I'm going to also try to do some character growth/change which I guess would be personal drama?  We love to see it.
responses.support_note: omg I would love some assistance I'm so bad at being manipulative and that's like what my new character is all about lol. I think some support in political schemes would be super helpful!
responses.court_motivation: Getting information for her sire; who is the most likely to succeed as prince, who has the prince's ear, learning their weaknesses and information that can be used against them and generally information on whether the Australian court is worth the trouble and risk
responses.ambitions_sydney: Establishing territory for her Sire (and herself), subtly trying to destabilise the court so no one can get a steady foothold. She intends to stay in Sydney as she is still considered a missing person in Tasmania and it's easier to blend into the crowd.
responses.why_sydney: She was ordered to by her sire the Brigadier-General as she's Australian and one of his more trusted neophytes from his territory in Mumbai, India. With information relayed by her he will decide if Australia is worth being conquered for Queen and country.
responses.why_covenant:
The Brigadier is Lancea so she is Lancea, and didn't even consider she had a choice, though she's discovering that the beliefs he taught her are a little different to her new covenant's. 

If she got to choose she would probably have chosen Crone...
responses.covenant_goals: Oh she knows she's on the bottom rung, but she intends on using this low status to fly under the radar while still having the protection of a strong covenant.
responses.clan_goals: One of the only good things about the Brigadier is that he made her strong and hard to kill- a gangrel. She embraces her heritage but is wary of others, especially elders. This doesn't mean she won't follow them- she's been used to taking orders, it's kept her alive for 25 years. Her main goal is to learn how to shift into animals like her sire.
responses.aspired_position: Indirect influence- a socialite.
responses.view_traditions: They're beneficial and should be necessary but they only work when you can be held accountable.
responses.view_elysium: Genuinely appreciates Elysium but is aware that we're all new to Australia and there are no traditionally sacred Kindred places here or real fear of consequences so it feels more like the kindred are just "promising" not to attack each other right now.
responses.view_mortals:
Mortals: food, entertainment, replaceable
Ghouls: she pities them and will treat them politely.
responses.embrace_story: Lured by a neophyte and brought to the Brigadier's villa, Tegan was fed on and turned because the Brigadier didn't have "an Australian". It was by choice; she could choose to be Embraced, or die.
responses.sire_story:
Brigadier-General Charles Tenley, active. 
He collects tourists from all over the world and uses them to carry out his whims but not many make it past the neophyte stage. Tegan was embraced because she was likeable but she's still around because she's manipulative. She's scared of him but respects him, he is indifferent to her but has convinced her that she needs him to survive.
responses.early_nights: Mumbai. She was embraced into a "pack" who all shared the same sire and the same rivalry. The city of Mumbai is always busy, loud, bright; totally overwhelming and she had to hit the ground running if she wanted to survive. All manner of man and beast passes through the "Gateway to India" so she has never had to worry about going hungry.
responses.last_city_politics: There is a court in Mumbai but the Brigadier chooses not to take part so neither do his neophytes. Instead he has a territory that he fiercely protects, with a haven hidden on the wooded coast. The Mumbai court do not venture into it and he stays out of theirs (though sends his neophytes into the city). For Tegan, the Brigadier is judge, jury, and executioner. Plus father, teacher, preacher, and boogeyman.
responses.touchstones: The smell of pine: Reminds her of exploring the forests with her sister and dad as a kid.
responses.hunting_style_note:
Takes advantage of inebriated people, mostly drunk people who she can convince to "check out a new place where the bar won't cut you off" or high people at raves/clubs/parties. 

Preferred territory is the city, she's learned to use dark alleys but also busy streets. 

Uncrossable lines: 
Can't feed on cows.
responses.first_kill:
Loss of control. 

She had been banished from the haven (a common occurrence for the Brigadier's neophytes) and was surviving in the forests surrounding it when she came across a kine. She doesn't know what they were doing there because they didn't seem to be camping, and she didn't mean to kill them, but she was hungry and angry and didn't realise her own strength. She buried him on the same night and doesn't feel too guilty about it. Girl's gotta eat.
responses.common_indulgences: Bushwalking/hiking, camping, birdwatching, partying, festivals and visiting zoos/wildlife parks.
responses.allies: Not really.
responses.coterie: No.
responses.enemies: Not at the moment.
responses.opposed_covenant: She knows she should oppose the Crone but secretly she's very curious about them.
responses.intolerable_behaviours: ? Animal abuse but even animal indifference can rile her.

mortal_family.description: Father, mother, and younger sister (now 48) are alive in Tasmania. Tegan has had zero contact since 2000 and is still on the Tasmanian missing persons list. She stalks them on social media and knows she'll break the masquerade if she reveals herself to them.

responses.high_concept: The Shadow where your eyes wont look.
responses.gaming_style_note: I want political chess not edgy cliques. Personal horror, world building, complexity and nuance. PvP is ok. Im curious how the mechanics will intersect with roleplay, and Im keen to court within the clan rather than push out against others...
responses.support_note: The story tellers have a balancing act. I would like some guidance probably with the application of rules in a complex encounter so that "everything stays in character"
responses.court_motivation: The Mammon Cult is an important resource for the Carthian Movement, but the Sydney chapter is considered groundwork. I am also being manipulated to follow/shepherd the Crone La Rois
responses.ambitions_sydney: To protect the touchstone. To establish Mammon/3Finkers within Australian Financial Elites. Sydney itself is of no personal connection. I am here ostensibly for Carthian business
responses.why_sydney: Extension of the successful Mammon cult is an important outcome in this new court. this is seen as important covenant groundwork for future expansion as the blood potency cap fades. However I am actually following - at a distance - the Crone LeRoux, whom I secretly support
responses.why_covenant: It was practical. Though my personal conviction to the convenants loftier goals are essentially lip-service, the Carthians were the only place for a displaced wretch like myself to find safety. Over time, the mutual utility became strong alignment to the agenda of allies who happen to be Carthian. Friends have always been seen as more useful than enemies.
responses.covenant_goals: The Carthian mess is perfect as it is. So I am motivated to resist internal agendas that want to "fix" it.
responses.clan_goals: My social connections to my clan are weak at best. My sire is truly dead (at my hand - but this memory was wiped by the Mnemosyne). My standing within my original Invictus line was poisoned by conspiracy. "Punished" by staking and torpor and imprisonment for 34 year before release in the 1950's. I was briefly returned to the Constantinople Court (now Istanbul) as an Invictus of quite low status, before being manipulated to move to the Louisiana USA court, again by the Mnemosyne in 1978. I have true contempt for Invictus, personally Amaranthed my sire (though i have no memory of this) and actively seek separation from them. The touch of my Sire's allies and coterie is a constant threat, deeply coupled to the tragic interwoven stories of myself, my great-granddaughter (Crone LaRoux)
responses.aspired_position: Enforcer
responses.view_traditions: Masquerade - super important. Progeny - in my sad experience a political tool weaponised by elites to shape power and wreak havoc. Amaranth - the end goal. this one is complicated. not against this one. secretly feels it is inevitable. lets not bring it up any further. "I think we all agree it is frowned upon in the strictest terms..."
responses.view_elysium: Absolutely, when it is in place. I work within/around the prevailing power structure whatever it was.
responses.view_mortals: They are tools, useful and most effective when properly managed. Manipulate them most effectively with Honey not vinegar. But for the most part I like to keep my cult followers without powers.
responses.embrace_story:
Upon my wedding day, in the midst of war. 
We married with some urgency because my wife, a Daeva was freshly pregnant with my child. She had designs to Sire me as a Daeva on my wedding night after consumating the marriage/ritual. I had no real idea, merely euphoric at the attention and fascination of a beautiful woman. 
As she finally closed to embrace me a _dominate_ effect dropped, and from the shadows stepped my soon to be actual SIre (a Nos). As an act of inter-Invictus rivalry and political spite I was embraced in front of my bride on our wedding bed.
responses.sire_story:
My sire is/was an Invictus Nosferatu (Venkat Haten) in the Turkish court in Constatinople in 1896. After my embrace, my wife was enraged and conspired with her Sire for revenge, and I was chosen by her father to be the tool for it. I was dominated and assisted to Amaranth my Sire, and taken for punishment. A nice tidy bow on the whole sordid family shame.

The state of the court in the war was such that boons flowed, my wife petitioned for clemency, and rather than being executed I was staked, boxed and buried - at least until such time as the inter-Invictus machinations settled. My wife was not told and believed me dead. This remained the case for over 5 decades and by the time the war was over the records were lost. Discovered after WWII, my casket was judged/assessed by the Mnemosyne who decided that this tragic story was far from complete... and had some wicked games. My memory of the Amaranth was wiped. But the reasons for my deep grudge were left clear and unresolved. An open wound. I was returned to the court and subtly hinted that I should pursue my goals away from Invictus with a fresh start in the New World.
responses.early_nights: Constantinople (1896), Torpor (1896-1950), Constantinople (1950-78), Louisiana USA (1978-2025)
responses.last_city_politics: The Louisiana court is famously stable, with a very strong Crone presence there.
responses.touchstones:
A portrait of my wife that was intended as a wedding gift. A precious keepsake. Looking at it is agony of the most perplexing kind.

A have an actual human touchstone. A turkish washerwoman with a dirty dry cleaning operation running near Macquarie Street. She reminds me of the a cantankerous particular villager from the old world, and I listen to her incessant compliants like I am mainlining human experience. I go out of my way to ensure she always has things to complain about but she is definitely protected from any actual harm from normal threats.
responses.hunting_style_note: they are manipulated into offering up their own necks in the pursuit of their own interests. Almost always unseen, eye shut, trembling but technically willing
responses.first_kill:
My first feeding was my first kill. My sire. It was not me, not frenzy - I was a passenger in someone elses frenzy played out through me. If I kill during feeding it was lack of care. Bane: Plague of Purity shields against Premeditated murder - but it is really a lack of care.

But in general, I feed from my cult, and will be extremely careful NOT to kill them. They are a precious resource.
responses.common_indulgences: Eavesdropping, Peeping-tom, Voyeur
responses.allies: I recognise Mnemosyne but am not aware of my role in his play. I am fixated upon Crone LaRoux, but she will likely never know that I hover... Ive kept it quite for a lot of years already.
responses.coterie: Not presently.
responses.enemies: Not presently, but there seem to be some Invictus aligned Carthians that are deeply suspicious... Any Invictus will likely require thwarting at some point.
responses.opposed_covenant: Absolutely. Invictus will hammer themselves upon the anvil of lies I feed them.
responses.intolerable_behaviours: Spoilers. Vandals. Tantrums. Crude Frenzy. I find such things unsubtle and crass. It bad enough that I will likely take subtle action.

mortal_family.description: My Daeva wife gave birth to a Damphir that was quickly sent at arms length from the vampire court and raised by ghoul family. There are generations descended from her, all female. My great-granddaughter (Crone LaRoux) was turned by Gangrel in 1978 and was sent to Sydney by the Louisiana Crones. She is the spitting image of my WIFE, and identical to my touchstone portrait of my wife from my wedding day.

boons_debts.description: coming in clean.
